news.Title: Elon Musk Has Never Sold His Tesla Vision. Should You Buy the Dip Before July 22?
news.Content: Say what you will about Elon Musk , but he has never blinked on his Tesla vision. Through years of skeptics declaring Tesla (NASDAQ: TSLA) was doomed, he kept promising a future of electric cars, self-driving fleets, and humanoid robots, and he kept plowing money back into those bets. Missed Nvidia in 2009? This Rare Signal Is Flashing Again. In 2009, a "Double Down" signal flashed for a little-known chipmaker called Nvidia. For the first time in years, that same "Total Conviction" signal is flashing for a company 1/100th the size of Nvidia. Continue » With the stock slipping below $400 and second-quarter earnings due July 22, some investors are wondering whether this dip is the moment to buy into that conviction. The founder-led lens There is a reason founder-led companies command loyalty, and Tesla is the textbook case. Musk holds an enormous personal stake and has worked to increase his voting control, which means his fortune rises and falls with the same shares ordinary investors own. Rather than harvesting profits, he keeps funneling them into ambitious projects: the robotaxi rollout, the Dojo supercomputer, the Optimus robot, and the energy business. To believers, that relentless reinvestment amid constant criticism is the whole point. It signals an owner playing a decade-long game while Wall Street frets over the next quarter. Founders who refuse to sell their vision have, more than once, been proven right long after the doubters moved on. The July 22 reality check That is the romantic case. The sober one is that Tesla's most recent quarter showed the tension clearly. Deliveries actually beat expectations, yet the stock fell as investors focused on shrinking margins, softening sales in North America, and the heavy spending required to chase all those moonshots. The stock still trades at a valuation that assumes the autonomous future arrives more or less on schedule, and Musk's timelines have a long history of slipping. Buying specifically to front-run one earnings report is less an investment than a coin flip, because a single print can swing hard in either direction. The takeaway for investors So should you buy the dip before July 22? I would gently separate the two questions tangled up in that headline. Believing in Musk's founder-led vision is a legitimate, decade-long call, and plenty of investors are comfortable backing a leader who has never wavered. But timing a purchase to a specific earnings date is a different, riskier game, and Tesla's lofty valuation leaves little cushion if the report disappoints. Story Continues If you are putting hard-earned savings to work here, the better question is not "before or after July 22," but whether you are willing to own an expensive stock through years of volatility on the strength of a vision that is still unproven. Decide that first, and the earnings date matters a lot less. Missed Nvidia in 2009? This Rare Signal Is Flashing Again In 2009, a "Double Down" signal flashed for a little-known chipmaker called Nvidia. If you'd invested $5,000 then, you'd be sitting on $2,759,194 today.* Now, for the first time in years, that same "Total Conviction" signal is flashing for a company 1/100th the size of Nvidia. It's a key player in the $1.8 trillion space race, and with the stock recently sitting 20% off its highs, the window to get in early is closing fast. Continue » *Stock Advisor returns as of July 13, 2026 Micah Zimmerman has no position in any of the stocks mentioned. The Motley Fool has positions in and recommends Tesla. The Motley Fool has a disclosure policy . Elon Musk Has Never Sold His Tesla Vision. Should You Buy the Dip Before July 22? was originally published by The Motley Fool Image source: The White House. news.Title: 台股崩跌2953點創史上最大跌點！盤整前5大股災
news.Content:
【緯來新聞網】全球晶片股遭遇拋售潮，台股今（17）日在美股半導體股重挫及台積電法說會後賣壓湧現下全面潰堤，加權指數開低走低，盤中接連跌破45000點、44000點及43000點三道整數關卡，終場暴跌2953.22點，收在42671點，跌幅6.74，同步改寫台股史上最大盤中與收盤跌點紀錄，成交值達1.21兆元。

台股示意圖。（圖／擷取自Pixabay）

市場恐慌情緒快速蔓延，權值股全面倒地。台積電雖在法說會上繳出優於預期的財測並上調全年展望，但市場擔憂AI投資持續擴大、資本支出增加恐壓縮未來獲利，加上台積電ADR前一日下跌2.32，拖累現股重挫180元、收2290元，跌幅7.29，創下單日最大跌點，單一個股即拖累大盤約1400點。

電子股成為重災區，聯發科大跌330元、跌幅8.92，台達電跌8.66，聯電、南亞、國巨等權值股同步跌停。半導體供應鏈更掀起跌停潮，晶圓代工、記憶體、封測、PCB及ABF載板族群幾乎全面亮起綠燈，華邦電、南亞科、力積電、群聯、晶豪科、愛普等記憶體股全面重挫，南電、欣興、景碩等載板股同步遭摜殺。

盤整前5大跌點

2026/07/17 跌點2953.71 指數收盤42671.27

2025/04/07 跌點2065.87 指數收盤19232.35

2024/08/05 跌點1807.21 指數收盤19830.88

2026/06/26 跌點1683.50 指數收盤44571.76

2026/06/08 跌點1568.16 指數收盤43502.78

根據證交所統計，原紀錄2025年4月7日，當時美國總統川普宣布全球性「對等關稅」，引爆全球股災，加權指數重挫2065.87 點；如今台股終下重摔近3千點，一舉超越前次紀錄。

news.Title: AI熱潮疑慮加劇 科技股領跌日經挫4%
news.Content:
（中央社東京2026年07月17日綜合外電報導）市場對於全球人工智慧（AI）熱潮能否持續的疑慮升高，東京股巿日經225指數今天在科技類股領跌下收盤重挫4.03%。

news.Title: 台股重挫2953點最大跌點! 跌破4萬3季線失守
news.Content:
[ 非凡新聞 ]記者林浩宇

台股周五遭遇今年以來最猛烈的賣壓。台積電法說會才剛釋出樂觀看法，市場原本期待出現「法說行情」，沒想到今（17）日卻遭遇「黑色星期五」，電子權值股全面走弱，半導體供應鏈更成為殺盤重心，加權指數終場大跌2953點，寫下史上收盤第一大跌點，收在最低點42671點，失守45000、44000、43000三道整數關卡，成交量1.2兆元。

《非凡新聞》記者 林浩宇：「護國神山法說會行情難道失靈了嗎？法說會後第一個交易日，指數不漲反跌，一開盤就重挫，摜破45000點，以及44000點兩道整數大關。」

盤中跌勢持續擴大，下跌超過2900點，創下史上第一大盤中以及收盤跌點紀錄，不只月線守不住，季線也同步跌破，明明台積電法說會釋出正面訊息，大盤卻依舊不給力。

統一投顧董事長 黎方國：「昨（16）日的美國股市，半導體股還在大跌，尤其是記憶體相關的個股也是重挫，加上今（17）日韓國股市休市，連帶日本股市、台灣股市，整個跟半導體相關的個股都是大跌。我想短線的下跌是籌碼面跟過度擁擠交易的結果，籌碼面的疑慮或股價修正之後，股市就回歸基本面。」

電子權值股首當其衝，護國神山台積電股價跌破代號2330元，鴻海同步走弱，台達電、聯發科跌幅超過半根停板，日月光更亮燈跌停，五大權值股全面承壓。

民眾：「蠻慘的，跌幅大概多少，應該5%以上吧，已經看開了，套蠻高的所以就放著吧。」

民眾：「今天不開了不開APP了，（持股）都是權值股，就台積電、聯發科、鴻海、聯電之類的，50000、50000，今年底50000。」

股民替台股加油，因為盤面電子、金融、傳產通通下挫，台積電概念股，辛耘、志聖、昇陽半導體一片綠油油；聯電、世界先進雙雙跌停；被動元件族群國巨、華新科也亮綠燈；ABF載板、記憶體全都倒地。

亞洲投顧分析師 吳岳展：「很多個股已經下跌30%到40%了，這個位階已這樣的跌幅來看，對於融資的去槓桿，大概會達到一定的效果，所以我覺得在止跌的位置，在季線附近可能就有機會，如果有機會恐慌殺到42000的話，可能會是個更好的位置。」

分析師認為，這波修正是短線籌碼整理，並非基本面反轉，雖然市場震盪但成交量還是高，證交稅持續挹注國庫，今年稅收有望挑戰3兆元，反映市場交易動能仍在，等待籌碼沉澱、投資信心回穩，台股將會重新站穩腳步。（記者林浩宇、林秋強／台北報導）

news.Title: 《放．新聞》台股史上最慘！收盤暴跌2953點創最大跌點　台積電重挫7.29%、一天拖垮大盤逾1300點
news.Content: 台股今（17日）遭遇史上最慘烈的一個交易日。 加權指數開低走低，終場以全日最低點42671.27點作收，暴跌2953.71點、跌幅6.47%，同步改寫台股盤中與收盤最大跌點紀錄。四萬五、四萬四及四萬三三道關卡在同一天內接連失守，盤勢一路探底至收盤，完全沒有出現明顯反彈。全日成交金額達1兆2129.57億元。 這場「717股災」的殺盤核心，正是前一天才交出史上最強成績單的 台積電 。 台積電16日召開法人說明會，公布第2季稅後純益7065.62億元，每股盈餘27.25元，毛利率67.7%，均創下單季歷史新高；營收也達1兆2703.81億元。 然而，亮眼財報未能支撐股價。台積電今日開盤即重挫，終場收在2290元，下跌180元、跌幅7.29%，不但寫下公司史上最大單日下跌金額，更拖累大盤超過1300點。 史上最強財報卻換來暴跌 市場開始算AI投資的代價 台積電法說會釋出的長線訊號依然強勁。公司上修全年展望、加大資本支出，董事長魏哲家也持續看好AI需求。 但市場短線在意的，已不只是AI需求有沒有成長，而是如此龐大的資本支出，究竟要多久才能轉化為足夠的獲利。

news.Title: 日經指數週五重挫4.03%，鎧俠、東京威力科創、三菱日聯股價黯淡
news.Content:
【財訊快報／劉敏夫】日本股市週五走跌，因華爾街拋售潮引發市場對AI熱潮能否持續的疑慮。收盤時，東證第一部指數下跌109.58點或2.72%，報3,919.21點；日經225指數則是下滑2,694.42點或4.03%，報64,141.12點。

近期科技股表現疲軟，因投資人對於AI熱潮能否持續抱持高度憂心。週四追蹤科技股的那斯達克100指數收跌1.62%；追蹤半導體的費城半導體指數收跌4.29%。

Sumitomo Mitsui DS Asset Management首席市場策略分析師Masahiro Ichikawa表示，儘管半導體相關股票的盈利報告強勁，但市場仍然賣出，這表明市場已經消化了這些強勁的業績，從而導致了利多出盡的反應。

大和證券首席策略分析師Yugo Tsuboi則是表示，日股此次拋售的幅度遠比美國市場看到的要大得多。由於韓國股市週五休市，投資者們還來不及消化韓國半導體行業的擔憂，所以有些人可能會轉而利用日本股市進行對沖。

個股表現方面，記憶體晶片製造商鎧俠股價下跌16.10%；半導體製程設備生產商東京威力科創股價下滑8.17%；營收逾五成來自北美市場的汽車製造大廠豐田汽車股價滑落0.33%；金融控股業者三菱日聯金融集團股價下降4.30%；能源開採商國際石油開發帝石股價下滑0.82%。

news.Title: 【盤後焦點】台積電跌破季線　台股慘跌2953點創史上最大跌點
news.Content:
（中央社記者江明晏台北2026年7月17日電）台股今天重挫2953.71點，改寫史上最大跌點紀錄，終場收在最低42671.27點，跌破季線43525點關卡。權值股成今天殺盤重心，台積電 (2330) 跌180元，收2290元，跌幅7.29%，聯電 (2303) 、國巨 (2327) 、南亞 (1303) 跌停，聯發科 (2454) 、台達電 (2308) 跌近9%。

人工智慧（AI）相關企業股價下跌，拖累全球股市表現，美國股市主要指數今天全面收黑。費城半導體指數重挫4.29%。日股盤中重挫逾5%，尾盤跌幅收斂。

台股今天以45234點開低後，指數一路震盪走低，跌點不斷擴大；終場在台積電尾盤重挫下，指數收在今天最低點42671.27點，下跌2953.71點，改寫歷史最大跌點紀錄。

晶圓代工廠台積電法人說明會落幕，儘管第2季財報報喜，但股價賣壓沉重，盤中跌破季線2325元，尾盤再遭逾9000張大單摜壓，終場下跌180元，創下史上最大單日跌點，收在2290元，跌幅7.29%。權值股聯電、國巨、南亞跌停，聯發科、台達電下跌近9%。

台股今天多殺多賣壓湧現，上市櫃合計1770檔下跌，跌停家數134檔。AI指標股載板族群也重挫，南電 (8046) 、欣興 (3037) 、景碩 (3189) 、臻鼎 (4958) 均跌停；記憶體族群華邦電 (2344) 、南亞科 (2408) 、力積電 (6770) 、群聯 (8299) 股價也跌停重挫。

以類股表現來看，上市機電類股重挫7.38%、塑化大跌6.53%，八大類股僅食品股上漲0.31%；上市電子及半導體指數分別重挫7.4%及7.71%。

觀察高價股走勢，股王信驊 (5274) 跌6.87%，高價股包括旺矽 (6223) 、弘塑 (3131) 、雍智 (6683) 等多檔跌停，昇達科 (3491) 抗跌為千金股唯一守住紅盤的個股；兆聯 (6944) 、萬潤 (6187) 、旭隼 (6409) 今天失守千元，台股千金股降為46檔。（編輯：張良知）

news.Title: 台股狂挫近3千點！雪崩原因曝光　分析師解讀基本面全說了
news.Content:
即時中心／徐子為報導

昨（16）天召開法說會的台積電（2330），今天台股開盤後一路下殺，終場股價下滑180元、收在2,290元。對此，分析師蔡明翰指出，這波跌勢並非基本面惡化，而是上半年漲幅過大後，市場出現估值與籌碼修正，公司展望本身仍偏正向。

分析師：台積電法說會高於市場預期 前半年「漲太多」就是最大利空

台股今（17）天跳空開低，指數不斷下行，終場指數就以42,671.27點作收、下跌2,953.71點，創下史上最大跌點；跌幅6.47％，成交量1兆2,129.57億元。

蔡明翰指出，其實昨天台積電法說，無論財報、未來展望或大幅提高資本支出，都優於市場原先預期。

既然台積電法說會釋利多，那為何股價沒連動？他認為是「上半年真的漲太多」，上半年累積漲幅太大，短線股價與均線、獲利基本面的乖離也拉得太開。今年上半年費半指數與韓國股市漲幅都超過100%，台股漲幅也超過五成。在如此龐大的漲幅之後，市場對利多的反應門檻自然大幅提高。即使財報很好，也可能因為股價早已提前反映，出現「利多不漲、反而獲利了結」的情況。

蔡舉例，近期美光與三星就是最典型的例子。兩家公司財報好到不可思議，但財報公布後股價仍然持續重挫。這不代表好財報是假的，也不等於AI與記憶體多頭已經結束，而是短線股價跑得太快、乖離率過高。當所有人都已經提前買進，財報公布後就容易出現利多出盡與籌碼鬆動。

他分析，這次台股下跌的核心，不是基本面突然翻空，而是第二季以來台股、費半與韓股漲幅過大。市場進入修正時，往往不管獲利好不好，先殺漲最多的股票。

在這個階段，「漲多」本身就是最大的利空。

蔡明翰在該文結尾提醒，投資不是看到大家跑，就立刻跟著跑；而是要確認「主人」有沒有改變方向。只要EPS仍然向上，短線跑得太快、現在回頭找主人的那隻狗，最後仍會重新跟上基本面的腳步。

《民視新聞網》提醒您：內容僅供參考，投資人於決策時應審慎評估風險，並就投資結果自行負責。

原文出處： 快新聞／台股狂挫近3千點！雪崩原因曝光 分析師解讀基本面全說了

news.Title: 韓股波動急升創亞洲金融危機來之最，槓桿踩踏外溢、晶片股逼近熊市
news.Content:
【財訊快報／陳孟朔】主導亞洲股市上半年漲勢的AI與半導體動量交易正快速逆轉，韓國成為這波去槓桿風暴的震央。外資撤離、散戶融資接盤與單一個股槓桿ETF被迫再平衡形成連鎖賣壓，韓股波動急升至亞洲金融危機以來罕見水準，跌勢並向台灣、日本及美國晶片股擴散，顯示6月可能已成為本輪亞洲科技股動量行情的階段高點。首爾綜合股價指數(KOSPI)週四重挫6.37%，收在6,820.60點，較6月19日盤中締造的史上高點9,385.59點相比，急瀉2,564.99點或27.3%，不到一個月即跌入技術性熊市，韓股週五因國定假期休市。與此同時，費城半導體指數自6月22日高點回落約19%，逼近20%的熊市門檻，30檔成分股自高點以來全數下跌，顯示韓國市場的賣壓已演變為全球晶片股的同步估值修正。

韓股脆弱性來自資金結構失衡。外資在高檔持續減碼，散戶則透過融資與兩倍做多ETF承接三星電子和SK海力士，使漲勢期間的正向動量在行情反轉後變成強制賣壓。韓國與香港掛牌、連結兩大記憶體股的單股槓桿產品規模合計已達數百億美元，交易一度占KOSPI成交量約三成；由於產品每日必須恢復兩倍槓桿，標的下跌時須機械性賣出部位，造成股價越跌、ETF賣得越多的踩踏循環。

外部環境也不再有利動量交易。美伊戰火推升油價與避險美元，亞洲市場同時面臨能源成本上升、金融條件收緊及AI投資過度擴張疑慮；週五台灣加權指數暴跌2,953.71點或6.47%，締造史上收盤單日最大跌點，日經225指數盤中跌幅接近6%，中國的創指和科創50指數半日跌幅都超過4%。韓國監管機構雖已暫停新增單股槓桿ETF上市，並提高交易門檻，但相關措施短期可能加速既有槓桿部位縮減，意味市場底部將取決於外資賣壓和強制平倉何時告一段落，而非低估值本身。

news.Title: 韓股、日股光芒漸退，Michael Burry看好現在是逢低買入港股時機
news.Content:
【財訊快報／劉敏夫】外電報導指出，知名投資人Michael Burry認為現在正是香港股市尋找逢低買入機會，因港股在今年的全球AI浪潮中表現落後。Scion Asset Management創始人Burry週五在X上發文表示，現在正是關注香港廉價股票的好時機，隨著韓股、日股和Soxx的光芒逐漸褪去，這些股票應該會有良好表現。

本月稍早時加倉了京東股票的Burry的最新評論，正逢全球晶片股拋售加劇之際，投資者對AI公司的技術及高額支出能否帶來回報產生質疑。除了他之外，看好香港股票的隊列也在不斷擴大，摩根士丹利也建議投資者增持港股，一定程度受盈利樂觀情緒推動。

由於消費者支出疲軟以及對中國電商行業的信心減弱，市場情緒低迷，香港恆生指數今年已下跌4.9%。

相較之下，韓國基準股指飆升62%，主要得益於該國兩大晶片巨頭股價的上漲。日本日經225指數自2026年初以來已上漲26%，而交易代碼為SOXX的iShares半導體ETF上漲76%。

news.Content:
（中央社台北17日電）台北股市今天開低走低，收盤跌2953.71點，為42671.27點，跌幅6.47%，成交金額新台幣1兆2129.57億元。

加權指數開盤為45234.08點，盤中最高45234.08點，最低42671.27點；不含金融股指數38189.10點，跌2849.40點。

八大類股漲跌幅：泥窯股跌3.51%、食品股跌0.31%、塑化股跌6.53%、紡織股跌1.70%、機電股跌7.38%、造紙股跌1.48%、營建股跌1.22%、金融股跌0.84%。

news.Title: 台股史詩級重挫「該逃or抄底」？ 蔡明翰喊別慌：不同水位不同策略
news.Content:
台股暴跌失守季線，市場陷入極度恐慌。分析師蔡明翰強調，台積電法說大釋利多，如今重挫僅是漲多後的籌碼修正，「主人與狗」終會朝相同方向前進，投資人應依持股水位冷靜應對。

台積電法說其實優於預期

台股於17日面臨史詩級大跌，盤中一度重挫超過2600點，不僅直接摜破43000點整數大關，盤中上市櫃下跌家數更飆破2000家，跌停家數也超過120家，連被市場視為生命線的季線也宣告暫時失守。面對這波急殺，分析師蔡明翰在臉書發文表示，許多人因為台積電在法說會交出亮眼成績單後，股價卻反轉下殺而感到恐慌。不過他認為，此時最重要的不是先看股價，而是要釐清法說會究竟是利多還是利空。蔡明翰指出，台積電昨天的法說會不論是財報、未來展望還是大幅提高的資本支出，表現都優於市場原先的預期。甚至在法說會結束後，多家外資同步上修了台積電2026年的EPS，目標價也普遍跟著調高。他直言，如果今天台積電沒有下跌，大家聽完法說會根本不會恐慌。現在市場的恐慌情緒，純粹是因為股價下跌後，大家才回過頭去替跌勢尋找理由，並非基本面真的出現利空。

漲多就是最大利空

針對基本面沒變壞卻大跌的現象，蔡明翰解釋，最關鍵的原因在於今年上半年累積的漲幅實在太大，導致短線股價與均線、獲利基本面的乖離拉得太開。在如此龐大的漲幅之後，市場對利多消息的反應門檻自然會大幅提高，因此就算財報表現再好，也容易因為股價早已提前反映，而出現「利多不漲、反而獲利了結」的籌碼鬆動現象。他以近期美光與三星的走勢為例，這兩家公司的財報同樣好到不可思議，但公布後股價依然持續重挫。這並不代表好財報是假的，或是AI與記憶體的多頭行情已經結束，單純是短線股價跑得太快、乖離率過高。蔡明翰精闢總結，財報不好而下跌是基本面修正，而財報很好卻下跌，更多是漲多與籌碼的修正。只要企業獲利沒有反轉，當股價乖離率降溫、過熱籌碼消化完畢，市場最後仍會回到原本的獲利成長軌道。

長線趨勢依舊看好

蔡明翰進一步分析，從2024年以來台積電股價與未來12個月預估EPS的走勢來看，兩者的長期方向高度一致。雖然短線必然會因為市場過度樂觀或恐慌而出現脫鉤，但只要預估EPS持續上修，長線趨勢依舊看好。

AI基礎建設投資仍未見頂

在面臨該逃還是該買的抉擇時，蔡明翰認為真正關鍵的思考點只有一個，那就是AI需求到了2027年究竟還能不能繼續成長。如果認為AI棒球賽已經進入第八、九局，2027年雲端資本支出將不再成長，那麼此時確實應該降低持股。但如果相信2027年的AI資本支出仍會高於2026年，台積電的獲利也會持續成長，那麼這次漲多後回測季線，比較像是分批布局的機會，而不是長線行情的結束。他強調，投資不是看到大家跑就立刻跟著跑，只要EPS仍然向上，最後仍會重新跟上基本面的腳步。

活用五四三法則分批布局

針對不同持股水位的投資人，蔡明翰建議，目前持股偏高的投資人，現在不需要急著全面加碼，而是該趁著這波修正重新檢視手中的持股。今年上半年上市櫃公司累計營收年增約34%，如果手中個股的累計營收成長明顯低於這個市場平均，又缺乏未來獲利上修的依據，就應該優先汰弱留強。畢竟有數字支撐的公司在修正後較有機會再創新高，而只有題材、沒有業績的股票，在市場冷靜下來後，反彈勢頭可能相對弱勢。對於目前持股偏低的投資人，蔡明翰指出，如今指數跌破整理區間、回測季線且市場恐慌明顯升高，已經符合他之前提到的「543階梯布局法」第一筆進場條件。他建議將資金分成5筆，在市場恐慌時投入第一筆，當第一筆虧損達3%到4%時，再投入第二筆，以此類推。透過不猜測最低點、以分批的方式來有效控制風險。

（封面圖／翻攝自蔡明翰臉書）

news.Title: 台股重挫2953點失守季線　創盤中及收盤最大跌點

news.Title: 黑色星期五！台股終場慘崩2953點「創史上最大跌點」台積電收2290元
news.Content:
即時中心／綜合報導

晶片股賣壓沉重，加上美伊局勢升溫，美股週四（16日）收盤全面走低；亞股盡顯頹勢，今（17）日日經指數盤中大跌4130點，香港恒生指數及上海綜合指數也跌逾2％。台股今跳空開低，指數不斷下行，終場指數就以42,671.27點作收、下跌2,953.71點，創下史上最大跌點；跌幅6.47％，成交量1兆2,129.57億元。昨召開法說會的台積電（2330）同樣一路向下，終場股價下滑180元、收在2,290元。

電子權值股方面，鴻海（2317）收在234元，跌8.5元；聯發科（2454）收3,370元，下跌330元；廣達（2382）收325.5元，跌26.5元；台達電（2308）以1,740元作收，下滑165元；日月光投控（3711）跌68元，收614元；聯電（2303）下跌16元，收144元；大立光（3008）收在4,010元，跌250元。

金融方面，富邦金（2881）收在124.5元，跌0.5元；國泰金（2882）跌3元，收在94.3元；中信金（2891）以62.1元作收，下跌0.9元；兆豐金（2886）收在47.6元，上漲1元；華南金（2880）漲0.75元，收40.15元；第一金（2892）收34.1元，漲0.15元；合庫金（5880）以25.35元作收，下滑0.15元。

news.Title: 史上最慘！台股血腥屠殺2953點 台積電崩7％、南亞科等135家跌停
news.Content:
台股創下收盤最大跌點！美股4大指數全面重挫，海力士ADR崩跌13％，這波股災延燒到亞股，衝擊台股今（17）日暴跌，終場大跌2953.71點收42671.27點，成交量1.21兆。上櫃指數同步下殺逾7%，大型權值股、中小型股一片慘綠。

台積電昨（16）日舉辦法說會並公布2026年第二季財務報告，單季EPS衝27.25元，毛利率拉升至67.7％，雙雙超越原先公布的財測高標，但公司宣布將在美國亞利桑那州加碼投資1000億美元，大幅提高資本支出預算恐衝擊短期毛利率，台積電今（17）日大跌逾7%收2290元。

權值股倒一片，聯發科及台達電重挫逾8%，聯電、國巨、聯發科跌停，記憶體股倒一片，南亞科、華邦電、力積電等多檔跌停，台股今總計共約135家跌停。

鐘崑禎分析師建議，市場極度恐慌時所有持股不殺低，之後初步反彈時再汰弱留強。

台股16日收45624.98點，下跌6.61點或0.01%，總成交值9331.81億元。三大法人賣超430.47億元，外資賣超483.35億元，投信、自營商買超86.45億元及賣超33.58億元。

道瓊工業指數下跌105.32點，跌幅0.20%，收52553.32點；標普500指數下跌38.63點，跌幅0.51%，收7533.77點；那斯達克指數下殺387.28點，跌幅1.47%，收25,881.95點。費城半導體指數暴跌531.39點，跌幅4.29%，報11,867.50點。

news.Title: 台股迎黑色星期五 盤中血洗跌逾2100點
news.Content:
美股全面收黑，費城半導體指數更是重挫4.29%，亞股迎來黑色星期五，日股盤中一度狂瀉超過2900點，台股開低走低，盤中一度最低4萬3433點，急殺超過2100點。

萬寶投顧執行長王榮旭表示，「今（2026）年上半年漲多，而漲多之後，其實獲利的賣壓蠢蠢欲動，當這些這個財報公布出來，沒有更多的額外的驚喜，來刺激市場的買氣的時候，往往就會伴隨著獲利了結的賣壓出籠。」

專家分析，目前台股正往季線4萬3500點來回測，倘若無法守住季線，台股可能會下探到4萬2000點。至於週五個股盤面，只有零星族群逆勢抗跌，大多類股都走跌，尤其以科技股首當其衝，台積電在法說結束後雖然公布的財報亮眼，但股價卻難逃下挫走勢，最低一度跌到2360元、跌110元，讓許多投資人直呼法說會變成法會。

而其他個股，包含聯電和華邦電股價都跌停。不只台股劇烈崩盤，新台幣兌美元同樣疲弱，新台幣兌美元以32.25元開出，一度觸及32.325元、貶7.6分。

匯率專家李其展指出，「貶值的趨勢還沒有結束，之前看起來的盤整區間是31.7到32.2元，破了之後當然走入一個新的區間，但是目前如果外資續賣，然後國際股市還是偏弱的話，那當然外資還是會慢慢往外走。」

專家認為，新台幣兌美元匯率要終止續貶，得觀察美國升息題材，是否出現變數？假使不升息，使得資金缺少迅速退場壓力，台幣就會有較大的反彈空間。

news.Title: 台積電Q2營收年增36% 上修全年成長展望
news.Content:
MoneyDJ新聞 2026-07-17 13:40:44 數位內容中心 發佈

全球晶圓代工龍頭台積電(TSM.US)於2026年7月16日公布2026年第二季財報，單季營收402億美元，創歷史新高，年增36.0%，淨利為新台幣7065.60億元，稀釋後ADR每股盈餘4.31美元，高於市場預估的3.89美元。

台積電同日舉行法說會與電話會議，將2026年全年營收成長預期上調至略高於40%，高於先前接近30%的展望，並同步提高2026年資本支出計畫。

針對2026年第三季，台積電提出營收指引為446億至458億美元，延續先進製程出貨動能，產能配置也持續朝高單價與高毛利的先進節點調整。

在製程進度上，2奈米製程已進入商業化初步導入階段，並占晶圓營收3%，顯示最先進製程已開始帶來實際營收貢獻。

台積電Q3營收續拚高，雙率處高檔

台積電Q2雙率及獲利齊創高，每股賺27.25元

資料來源- MoneyDJ理財網

news.Title: 台積電法說利多卻遭無情摜殺逾6%跌破季線 市值蒸發近4兆元
news.Content:
台積電 (2330-TW)(TSM-US) 昨日召開法說會，儘管釋出樂觀展望，外資也多數上調目標價，不過市場不買單，今日早盤以下挫近 4% 開出，且跌幅不斷擴大，午盤過後大跌超過 6%，下探至 2,310 元，下探一個月來新低點，貢獻大盤跌點高達 1,200 點。

台積電盤中大跌逾 6% ，市值也從昨天的 64.05 兆元，降至約 60.1 兆元，單日盤中市值蒸發近 4 兆元。

台積電本次法說會釋出多項正面訊息，包括 2026 年美元營收年增幅由原先超過 30%，上調至超過 40%，並將全年資本支出提高至 600 億至 640 億美元，反映公司看好 AI 需求持續增強，能見度甚至可望延伸至 2029 年至 2030 年。

不過，市場焦點仍集中在毛利率與資本支出壓力。台積電第二季毛利率達 67.7%、營益率 60.3%，雙雙優於公司財測高標，但毛利率仍低於部分法人原先期待的 70%，使得財報雖然優於預期，卻未能帶來更大驚喜。

台積電也預估，2 奈米量產爬坡將稀釋 2026 年下半年毛利率約 3 至 4 個百分點，海外晶圓廠則可能再影響 2 至 3 個百分點。儘管公司強調，將透過生產效率提升、跨製程節點最佳化及紀律定價抵銷部分成本壓力，投資人仍擔憂未來獲利率可能自高檔回落。

另一方面，台積電宣布再增加 1000 億美元亞利桑那州投資，使美國總投資金額提高至 2650 億美元。雖然公司尚未公布新增廠區的明確建廠時程，但市場擔心海外廠成本較高，加上設備、材料與建廠費用上升，可能加重折舊與毛利率壓力。

外資對台積電長期展望仍普遍偏多，多家券商在法說會後上調獲利預估與目標價，目標價介於 3000 元至 4200 元之間，並多數維持買進評等。外資認為，資本支出提高反映 AI 訂單與先進製程需求強勁，而非盲目擴產，且台積電會同步檢視雲端業者的資料中心建設、電力供應及設備部署進度，以確認需求來自實際建置，而非庫存堆積。

news.Title: 3 High-Yield Dividend Stocks to Buy and Hold
news.Content: Broader equities have performed so well in recent years that the dividends companies pay haven't kept pace. As a result, the S&P 500 's average yield is just 1.1% right now. Thankfully, it's possible to find high-yield dividend stocks that are worth investing in. Here are three examples: Pfizer (NYSE: PFE), Novo Nordisk (NYSE: NVO), and Sanofi (NASDAQ: SNY). All three have faced some issues lately, but they are worth sticking with for the long ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Pfizer offers a forward dividend yield of 7.1% despite recent headwinds and upcoming patent cliffs.
• Key growth drivers for Pfizer include Padcev (cancer medicine) and Abrysvo (RSV vaccine), with promising pipeline developments in oncology and weight-loss.
• Novo Nordisk, a leader in diabetes and weight loss, has a strong pipeline including zenagamtide (amycretin) in Phase 3 studies for weight loss and CagriSema for diabetes/weight loss.
• Sanofi, despite recent headwinds and clinical setbacks, has long-term prospects driven by its key growth driver, Dupixent.
news.Keywords: high-yield dividend, patent cliff, pipeline, GLP-1, oncology

news.Title: Lucid Just Soared 29% After Calling Bankruptcy Rumors "Completely False." Here's What Its Balance Sheet Actually Shows.
news.Content: It was a wild two days for Lucid (NASDAQ: LCID) shareholders. On Tuesday, a report from an electric vehicle blog, citing two unnamed sources, claimed the luxury EV maker had brought in consulting firm AlixPartners to weigh a Chapter 11 bankruptcy filing or a take-private deal. Shares lost more than half their value at Tuesday's lows, triggering multiple volatility halts, before Lucid's denial helped the stock recover most of the damage. It still closed the day down 16%. ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Lucid's stock surged 29% after the company denied bankruptcy rumors, stating it has sufficient liquidity to operate well into next year.
• The company's pro forma total liquidity is approximately $4.7 billion, following a $1.05 billion raise in April and drawing $500 million from a PIF term loan.
• Lucid's first-quarter net loss was $1 billion, with operations consuming about $1.2 billion in cash and $253 million in capital expenditures.
• Despite improved deliveries and revenue growth (20% year-over-year to $282.5 million in Q1), the company's burn rate suggests its current liquidity covers just over three quarters.
news.Keywords: bankruptcy rumors, liquidity, cash burn rate, capital expenditures, EV market

news.Title: 台股重挫2600點 達人曝「未必壞消息」：能買到便宜台積電
news.Content:
台積電在法說會公布財報及財測，營收、EPS不只全面優於市場預期，更刷新歷史同期新高，但台積電ADR盤前一度大跌約5%，今（17）日盤中更是慘跌140元，來到2330元，影響大盤指數重挫近2600點，跌破43K關卡。

對此，投資達人葉育碩在臉書發文表示，很多人都很錯愕「財報這麼漂亮，為什麼還跌？」市場在交易的從來不是現在，而是預期。相信大部分投資人都認為台積電每一次都能持續繳出優於市場預期的成績，也就是說「超預期」，本身早就已經是市場的預期，「大家都知道會考100分，那100分還算驚喜嗎？」

「所以最後真正影響股價的，還是華爾街那些外資到底想怎麼做。」葉育碩舉例，前陣子三星七月初公布了一份非常亮眼、甚至可以說相當驚艷的財報，結果股價不但沒有慶祝，反而一路重挫，如果連三星這麼好的財報都能跌，那麼台積電即使交出亮眼成績單，也未必代表股價當天一定會立刻上漲。

葉育碩指出，當所有人都預期它會很好，那財報公布後，反而容易出現利多實現的賣壓。但這並不代表公司基本面變差，而是代表市場先前已經把很多好消息提前反映到股價裡。因此，股價的短期波動，很多時候只是市場情緒、籌碼，以及預期差的調整。

葉育碩說真正值得關注的，反而是法說會透露出來的訊息，例如全年營收預估再度上修、AI 需求依舊強勁、先進製程持續滿載、第三季展望依然樂觀，這些才是真正影響企業長期價值的核心。如果財報比預期更好，股價卻變便宜，那對長期投資人來說，這未必是壞消息，這可能是市場送來的一次機會。

葉育碩也幽默的表示，「如果外資這麼喜歡賣，看可不可以賣到2000以下，這樣咱們就能買到便宜的台積電了，讚讚讚」。

（封面圖／東森新聞）

【 本文 由 東森財經 提供，未經授權，請勿轉載！】

news.Title: 暫居台股史上第二大跌點！台積電市值蒸發逾3兆元
news.Content:
【緯來新聞網】美股半導體股遭遇拋售，費城半導體指數重挫4.29，再加上台積電法說會後市場擔憂資本支出擴大恐壓縮獲利，台股今（17）日遭遇猛烈賣壓，加權指數盤中一度暴跌2329點，最低來到43295點附近，不僅失守45000點、44000點兩道整數關卡，更創下台股史上第二大盤中跌點，上市公司總市值一度蒸發約7.6兆元。

台股示意圖。（圖／資料照）

權值股成為重災區，台積電雖在法說會釋出優於市場預期的財測，但ADR先跌2.32，拖累今日股價一度重挫120元、最低來到2350元，市值瞬間縮水逾3兆元。聯電、國巨、南亞等大型權值股更遭打入跌停，聯發科、台達電、鴻海同步走弱，市場信心明顯轉趨保守。

AI供應鏈全面潰散，ABF載板族群跌勢最重，南電、欣興跌逾8，景碩、臻鼎跌幅超過9；記憶體股華邦電、南亞科同步下挫，被動元件及高價股也全面承壓。盤中約1500檔上市櫃個股下跌，跌停家數逼近70家，信驊、穎崴、旺矽等千金股同步走低，旭隼、AES-KY、萬潤、均華、金像電等跌破千元，使千金股家數降至46檔。

法人分析，市場對AI產業高資本支出與獲利回收速度的疑慮升溫，加上地緣政治風險及全球科技股修正，短線台股波動恐仍將維持高檔。

news.Title: 股匯雙殺 台幣早盤貶值7.7分、創逾一年新低
news.Content:
MoneyDJ新聞 2026-07-17 12:46:26 陳怡潔 發佈

台灣市場今(17)日股匯雙殺，加權指數早盤狂殺逾2500點，台幣兌美元隨之爆量走貶，匯價跌破32.300元整數關卡，盤中價創下去年4月底以來的逾一年新低，中午則貶值7.7分，暫收32.326元。

美伊衝突越演越烈，加上台積電(2330)提高資本支出、毛利率低於市場預期引發疑慮，美股上日全面收黑，費半指數重挫逾4%，亞洲股市今日隨之下殺；匯市方面，避險情緒利多美元走勢，今日在東京匯市早盤，美元指數持堅，日圓兌美元則繼續徘徊在40年低點附近。

台灣市場今日股匯雙殺，台積電領跌之下，加權指數早盤狂殺逾2500點，台幣兌美元隨之爆量走貶，匯價跌破32.300元整數關卡，盤中最多貶值8.9分至32.338元，創下去年4月底以來的逾14個月盤中新低，中午則貶值7.7分，暫收32.326元。

根據台北外匯經紀公司報價，17日早盤台幣兌美元貶值7.7分，暫收32.326元，區間在32.250-32.338元，成交10.7億美元。

美元走軟 台幣早盤震盪小升1.1分

不畏避險情緒升高，台幣早盤放量升值9分

news.Title: SpaceX, Alphabet, and SK Hynix Are Quietly Flashing a Bullish Signal Investors Should Not Ignore
news.Content: Are we heading toward an artificial intelligence (AI) infrastructure spending cliff? Some investors and analysts have been saying so for a while now, predicting that the leaders in the AI industry, such as Nvidia (NASDAQ: NVDA), could see their financial results worsen significantly once it happens. However, there are plenty of signs suggesting that AI spending isn't slowing down at all and may actually accelerate over the medium ...
news.AI_Summary:
• AI infrastructure spending is projected to exceed $1 trillion by 2029, indicating continued acceleration rather than a slowdown.
• SpaceX is significantly increasing its AI segment capital expenditures, with $12.7 billion in 2025 and $7.7 billion through March 31, 2026, expecting to continue ordering Nvidia products.
• Alphabet announced an $80 billion equity capital raise for AI projects, with projected capex of $180-$190 billion in 2026 and further growth expected in 2027.
• SK Hynix committed approximately $743 billion to expand its High Bandwidth Memory (HBM) chip manufacturing capacity, a key component for AI workloads, signaling strong demand for Nvidia's GPUs.
news.Keywords: AI infrastructure, capital expenditure, HBM, GPU demand, semiconductor ecosystem

Regional markets took a weak lead-in from overnight losses on Wall Street, ...
news.AI_Summary:

• Despite stronger-than-expected earnings and an upbeat outlook from TSMC, its shares fell 5% due to a sharp increase in capital expenditure forecasts.

news.Title: 〈焦點股〉光通訊股綠光罩頂 聯鈞等4檔跌停、6檔重摔逾半根停板
news.Content:
台股今 (17) 日受美股四大指數收黑，加上亞股慘綠影響，盤中恐慌重挫一度超過 2,000 點，光通訊概念股受指數修正及 Lumentum(LITE-US)、Marvell(MRVL -US) 重挫影響，今日紛紛綠光罩頂重跌，包括聯鈞 (3450-TW) 亮燈跌停，股價下探 432 元跌破季線，前鼎 (4908-TW)、光環 (3234-TW)、眾達 - KY(4977-TW) 也跌停。

觀察盤面上，除了聯鈞跌停等 4 檔跌停，包括波若威 (3163-TW)、華星光 (3163-TW) 及統新 (6426-TW)、聯亞 (3081-TW) 也慘跌 8-9%，上詮 (3363-TW)、光聖 (6442-TW) 一度重跌 7% 以上。

光通訊族群在幾個月前仍被市場人人追捧的題材，輝達黃仁勳在今年 Computex 上也讚譽光通訊廠邁威爾會是下一家「兆美元級企業」，將美、台灣光通訊概念股題材燒到最旺，但隨著 7 月起美股震盪、台股修正下，光通訊族群卻成了市場最先被拿來開刀的目標，今日多檔遭下修重跌。

法人指出，光通訊產品在未來仍持續看好，但在下半年，市場將再度驗證基本面及出貨是否兌現，包括台積電 (2330-TW)COUPE 2026 量產、Intel 和 Broadcom 各自有相關產品發展方向，投資人需留意後續產業成長的動能，是否從出貨量轉向規格產品的升級，配合相關營收及業績，再逢低進場布局績優股。

news.Title: 台積電法說後多家外資上調目標價 最高飆到4200元
news.Content:
台積電 (2330-TW)(TSM-US) 昨日於法說會釋出兩上調、一加碼，包括上調全年營收年增率至 40%、上調全年資本支出至 600-640 億美元，以及加碼美國投資 1,000 億美元，外資也於法說會後紛紛出具報告並上調目標價，最高為澳系外資的 4,200 元。

外資認為，儘管市場短期對毛利率預估、美國投資增加等議題有所疑慮，但整體看法仍偏多，普遍認為台積電本次再度繳出「財報優於預期、展望同步上修」的成績，且 AI 需求正由 GPU 加速器進一步擴散至 CPU、客製化 ASIC 及網通晶片，成為推升未來數年營收與獲利的主要動能。

外資普遍看好台積電資本支出上調至 600 億至 640 億美元，並將其視為正面訊號，認為此舉反映台積電對 AI 需求與客戶訂單的信心，而非盲目擴產。台積電管理層也強調，擴產前會實際檢視雲端服務供應商的設備部署時程、資料中心建設進度及電力供應狀況，不會單純加總客戶提出的需求預測。

外資認為，這代表目前 AI 需求主要來自真實的資料中心建置，而非客戶提前拉貨或堆積庫存，需求能見度甚至已延伸至 2029 年至 2030 年。

除了 GPU 與其他 AI 加速器需求持續強勁，Agentic AI 也成為本次外資報告共同關注的焦點。隨 AI 架構由生成式 AI 逐步轉向代理式 AI，系統需要更多 CPU 負責資料處理、任務調度及模型協作，帶動 x86 與 Arm 架構 CPU 需求同步升溫。

其中一家外資直言，市場目前仍低估 Agentic AI 帶來的 CPU 成長空間，其 2026 年至 2027 年台積電每股純益預估因此高於市場共識約 4% 至 10%。另一家外資則指出，AI 需求已由 GPU 擴散至 CPU、客製化 ASIC 及網通晶片，需求來源更加多元，也降低台積電成長過度集中於單一產品的風險。

在先進製程方面，外資看好 N3、N2、A16 及 A14 持續成為台積電主要成長引擎。其中，N2 家族產能在 2026 年至 2028 年間，年複合成長率可能超過 70%，N3 產能也將持續快速增加，以支援新一代 AI 晶片。

台積電 A14 製程則預計 2027 年試產、2028 年量產，目前早期良率表現良好，客戶接洽進度也優於原先規劃。管理層更預期，A14 及後續 A13、A12 所形成的產品家族，市場規模可能比 N2 更大，產品生命週期也更長。

不過，毛利率仍是外資關注焦點。外資指出，台積電第二季毛利率雖達 67.7%，優於公司財測高標，但仍未達部分法人期待的 70%，加上 2 奈米量產初期、海外晶圓廠成本及原物料價格上升，可能壓抑後續毛利率表現。

台積電預估，N2 量產爬坡可能稀釋 2026 年下半年毛利率約 3 至 4 個百分點，海外廠則可能再稀釋 2 至 3 個百分點。不過，多家外資仍認為相關壓力可控，台積電可透過先進製程產品組合改善、產能利用率提升、生產效率優化及價格調整，維持約 65% 至 66% 的高檔毛利率。

先進封裝方面，外資認為 CoWoS 仍是目前 AI 半導體供應鏈最主要的瓶頸，甚至已限制部分客戶 AI 晶片出貨。台積電正持續加速擴充後段封裝產能，同時對英特爾 EMIB、EMIB-T 等替代技術持開放態度。

外資指出，前段晶圓製造與後段封裝屬於不同業務，即使部分客戶採用其他廠商的封裝方案，前段晶圓仍可能由台積電生產。因此，英特爾等業者分擔先進封裝需求，未必威脅台積電，反而有助解除客戶出貨瓶頸，進一步擴大台積電前段晶圓業務。

針對美國布局，台積電再增加 1000 億美元亞利桑那州投資，使美國總投資金額提高至 2650 億美元。外資認為，相關投資反映台積電對 AI 需求及美國客戶長期需求的信心，但部分資本支出增加也來自設備、材料及建廠成本上升，並非完全等同於實際產能增加。

news.Title: Down 26%, Is IBM Stock the Smartest Dividend Stock to Buy for the Second Half of 2026?

Krishna's acknowledgment came as IBM issued preliminary earnings results for the second quarter, warning that sales were ...
news.AI_Summary:
• IBM's CEO acknowledged faltering execution as customers shifted spending away from IBM towards memory and storage products ahead of anticipated price increases.

news.Title: 台積電法說會後股價重挫 分析師點出兩大關鍵
news.Content:
台積電 在16日舉行的法說會交出優於市場預期的成績單，股價卻不漲反跌，拖累台股今天(17日)盤中重挫逾2,000點，跌破44,000點大關。分析師指出，市場認為台積電第三季毛利率預估略低於預期，將法說利多視為「利多出盡」，再加上美、韓、日股市同步重挫及台股累積漲幅已大，促使市場出現技術性拉回。

受到美股前一晚收黑影響，費城半導體指數重挫4.29%，台積電ADR下跌2.32%，亞股今天同步走弱，日本股市盤中跌近4%、南韓股市跌逾6%。台股今天開低走低，盤中大跌逾2,000點，失守44,000點關卡，電子股重挫逾4%，上市櫃約1,500檔個股下跌。

權值股成為殺盤重心，台積電盤中大跌100元至2,370元，跌幅超過4%；聯電、國巨、南亞跌停，聯發科、台達電跌逾5%，鴻海同步走弱。AI相關載板族群與記憶體股賣壓沉重，南電、欣興跌逾8%，景碩、臻鼎跌幅超過9%，華邦電、南亞科等記憶體股同步下挫。

對於台積電法說表現亮眼，股價卻反向重挫，資深分析師李永年接受央廣訪問時指出兩大因素。首先是美國、日本及南韓股市近期同步走弱，拖累市場投資氣氛；其次是台積電第三季毛利率展望略低於預期。他說：『(原音)其實台積電法說會的內容大部分都是優於市場預期的，可是因為他的毛利率，預估第三季的毛利率是略微降低、不如市場預期，所以現在市場的反應就是把他當作利多出盡來反映，今天的台積電以及台積電概念股走勢也相當的疲弱。』

對於近期南韓股市連續重挫，李永年表示，主要是當地政府推動股市去槓桿化，導致資金流出，加上半導體類股承受沉重賣壓；美股也因半導體股走弱而回跌。不過，他認為台灣股市情況與韓、美不完全相同，今天的下跌有相當程度是受到國際股市拖累，台灣基本面並未出現重大改變。

另外，李永年也指出，台股近兩年累積漲幅相當可觀，尤其今年以來漲勢凌厲，在高檔進行技術性修正本屬正常現象，短線不排除仍有低點可見，但只要企業基本面沒有改變，這波回檔反而有機會讓中長線投資人留意布局時機。

匯市部分，新台幣匯率持續走弱，台北外匯市場新台幣兌美元匯率今天以32.25元開盤，貶0.1分，最低來到32.33元兑1美元。(編輯：許嘉芫)

news.Title: 2 Stocks Down 44% and 30% to Buy Right Now and Hold for the Next Decade
news.Content: Some of the best decade-long investments start as beaten-down brands that everyone has temporarily given up on. The trick is separating companies with a broken business from companies with a strong brand going through a rough patch. Two consumer goods giants fit that second description right now. Nike (NYSE: NKE) has fallen about 44% from its high, and Estée Lauder (NYSE: EL) sits roughly 30% below ...
news.AI_Summary:
• The article discusses Nike and Estée Lauder as potential long-term investments despite recent stock price declines.
• Nike's stock is down approximately 44% from its peak due to a strategic shift away from retail stores.
• Estée Lauder's stock has fallen about 30% due to challenges in China, travel retail, and thinning profit margins.
• Both companies are presented as 'wounded champions' or 'prestige beauty leaders on the mend' with strong underlying brands.
news.Keywords: long-term investment, turnaround potential, consumer brands, market downturn, brand equity

news.Title: 台股暴殺2000點大場面！南亞科、旺宏、聯電、南電...國家隊救援清單1次看 下一站4萬點？行家示警了：跌勢還沒完
news.Content:
韓股今（17）日休市，導致台日股市同淪外資提款機，台股更是出現踩踏慘況，不僅台積電(2330)急殺逾百元，大盤也一度重挫2188點至4萬3436點。資深分析師提醒，台積電與聯發科(2454)是外資的兩大提款機，處境最危險；國家隊則是力抗賣壓，鎖定急跌的記憶體3雄、聯電(2303)等彎腰撿。

資深分析師陳榮華表示，韓股在短短1個月內回檔近25%，跌回4月底的起漲點，相當台股當時的4萬點位置，台股今日也在台積電法說後向下劇烈轉折，提醒大盤可能連跌9~15個交易日，在慣性走勢被改變下，建議投資人別急著抄底，靜待止穩信號或下個轉折時點的到來。

他並表示，韓股今日休市，導致台日股市淪為外資提款機，而台韓股市同樣面臨典型的去槓桿循環。

加上全球基金向來將台韓視為「東北亞科技雙核心」，當韓股急跌，外資為應付贖回潮，反而會先砍流動性最好的台股權值股，讓台積電、聯發科(2454)等兩大台股巨頭，反而比SK海力士等南韓記憶體股更危險。

台積電昨回補10.07億元

此外，8大官股行庫為首的國家隊，持續呈現土洋對作，不畏外資的龐大賣壓，昨（16）日反手回補台股48.3億元，預料今日加碼力度可望再擴大，不排除再度突破百億元。

在昨日護盤標的部分，就金額觀察，仍以台積電買超10.07億元最多，記憶體、PCB也是承接重心，光是南亞科(2408)與旺宏(2337)，就分別買超9.77億元、6.64億元。

至於買超第4~10名部分，依序為臻鼎-KY(4958)6.35億元、南電(8046)5.79億元、欣興5.01億元、華邦電(2344)4.69億元、聯電4.68億元、聯發科3.15億元與群聯2.97億元。

news.Title: Why Abbott Stock Jumped Today
news.Content: Shares of Abbott Laboratories (NYSE: ABT) popped on Thursday after the healthcare products provider raised its full-year profit forecast. Missed Nvidia in 2009? This Rare ...

news.Keywords: earnings forecast, revenue growth, diagnostics, medical devices, shareholder returns

news.Title: 《日股早盤》日經狂瀉2,939點、鎧俠自高點腰斬
news.Content:
MoneyDJ新聞 2026-07-17 10:39:33 蔡承啟 發佈

美國半導體股昨日(16日)重挫，日本AI、半導體相關股聞訊暴跌，拖累日經225指數17日狂瀉近3,000點，早盤收盤(台北時間上午10點30分)大跌4.40%或跌2,939.06點，暫收63,896.48點。

日本股市下週一(7月20日)因適逢國定假日「海之日」將休市一天。

東證股價指數(TOPIX)早盤收盤下挫2.61%或跌105.34點，暫收3,923.45點。

截至早盤收盤為止，東證Prime上漲家數607家、下跌家數919家、維持平盤家數26家。東證Prime成交額為5兆6,472億日圓。

就類股表現來看，截至早盤收盤為止，全部33類股中、有12類股上漲，其中以海運業類股漲幅最大，其次依序為零售業、水產農林業和食品業。

根據MoneyDJ XQ全球贏家系統的報價顯示，截至日本股市17日早盤收盤(台北時間上午10點30分)為止，日圓相對於美元匯率貶值0.03%至162.42日圓兌1美元；日圓相對於美元匯率16日貶值0.1%，3個交易日來首度走貶。

就個股表現來看，NAND Flash廠鎧俠(Kioxia)早盤崩跌15.65%、暫收52,390日圓，和6月22日創下的歷史最高紀錄112,700日圓相比、股價已腰斬；晶片設備巨擘東京威力科創(TEL)早盤暴跌8.66%、測試設備商愛德萬測試(Advantest)早盤狂瀉10.80%、生產AI資料中心用光纖的Fujikura早盤暴跌7.37%、矽晶圓廠SUMCO早盤崩跌14.69%、MLCC廠太陽誘電早盤崩跌14.89%。

日本海運股逆勢上漲。日本郵船(NYK)早盤大漲2.98%、商船三井(MOL)早盤漲1.45%、川崎汽船(K-Line)早盤大漲2.67%。

(圖片來源：Shutterstock)

news.Title: SK海力士ADR溢價飆27% 資金改買「這檔ETF」單日吸金11億美元
news.Content:
隨著投資人紛紛透過 ETF 間接布局 SK 海力士，美國掛牌規模最大的韓國 ETF 迎來史上最大資金流入。由於 SK 海力士新上市的美國存託憑證（ADR）相較韓國本地股票存在顯著溢價，不少投資人轉而將 ETF 視為替代投資工具。

根據彭博彙整數據，貝萊德旗下、資產規模達 230 億美元的 iShares MSCI South Korea ETF( EWY-US ) ，週三 (15 日) 吸引逾 11 億美元資金流入，前一日也創下單日 8.14 億美元的歷史新高。

該 ETF 約四分之一的持股配置為在韓國上市的 SK 海力士，因此成為海外投資人布局該股最便捷的管道之一。

Strategas Securities ETF 首席策略師 Todd Sohn 表示，ETF 本質上就是一種「代理投資（proxy play）」工具，無論是新興市場或成熟市場的投資主題，都能以極高效率取得市場曝險。

這波資金湧入發生在 SK 海力士 ADR 登陸美股後不久。該公司 ADR 上市首週便引發市場熱烈交易，價格大幅高於韓國本地股票。截至紐約時間週四下午，SK 海力士 ADR 較韓國股票的溢價約為 27%，前一天更一度衝上 51%，創下歷史新高。

相較於直接追價買進溢價較高的 ADR，部分投資人選擇透過 EWY 間接持有韓國上市的 SK 海力士股票。相較於直接投資韓股，ETF 不僅免去跨時區交易的不便，也省去外匯兌換等程序。

Jonestrading Institutional Services 股票銷售交易員兼總體策略師 Dave Lutz 表示，投資人正利用 EWY 作為投資韓國上市 SK 海力士股票的替代工具。

SK 海力士 ADR 與韓股之間的價差之所以持續存在，是因為通常可用來平抑兩地價格差異的轉換機制，目前仍受到限制。由於 ADR 的新增發行與註銷機制將持續關閉至本月底，投資人目前無法自由在 ADR 與韓國股票之間進行轉換。即使之後重新開放，市場仍不確定實際可提供的轉換額度，以及是否還需取得額外監管核准。

類似的 ADR 溢價現象也出現在台積電 ADR( TSM-US ) 。由於台積電台灣股票與 ADR 並非完全可相互轉換，根據彭博統計，過去一年台積電 ADR 平均較台股現貨溢價約 20%。

這波資金湧入，也反映韓國記憶體族群已成為今年 AI 投資熱潮中最受矚目的標的之一。儘管 SK 海力士赴美上市後股價波動加劇，資金仍持續流入相關族群。在韓國，散戶瘋狂追捧與 SK 海力士及三星電子掛鉤的單一股票 ETF，甚至促使監管機關一度暫停核准新的單一股票 ETF 上市。

Facet 投資長 Tom Graff 表示，部分資金流入可能來自空頭回補，但整體市場需求依然十分強勁。至於這股熱潮能否持續，仍有待觀察，不過在此情況下，多數放空部位迅速回補並不令人意外。

根據彭博統計，今年以來 EWY 已累計吸引逾 63 億美元資金流入，基金資產規模同期暴增超過 180%，目前已達約 230 億美元。

news.Title: 韓股波動難解！分析指限制槓桿ETF效果有限，Kospi恐續劇烈震盪
news.Content:
【財訊快報／劉敏夫】外電報導指出，金融媒體分析指稱，韓國暫停新的單一股票槓桿ETF上市，對遏制韓國Kospi指數波動作用不大，因為這一措施未能解決現有產品引發市場劇烈波動的問題。分析還指出，另有一個措施是，將最低存入金額要求從1,000萬韓元提高到3,000萬韓元，這雖會阻礙一部分散戶投資者進入市場，但本身並不足以平息市場波動。槓桿和散戶投資者的組合在任何市場中最終都很少會有好的局面，而眾多單一股票ETF的存在更加劇了這種情況。

在韓國當局採取更大膽的措施之前，日內波動加劇將成為Kospi指數的特點。

news.Title: 〈台股開盤〉崩跌逾1700點空襲季線 美日股齊挫 台積電法說利多反跌近百元
news.Content:
台積電 (2330-TW) 今 (17)) 日開盤重挫超過 3.5%，權重股的下跌並造成早盤大盤下跌超過 1700 點，大盤指數瀕臨約 43545 點的季線保衛戰， 同時，韓國股市因制憲節休市、日股早盤下跌逾 3.55% 也帶給台股極大的壓力；早盤台股電子股並重挫 3.7%。

台股大盤指數早盤指數以 45234.08 點開出，但也遭逢市場沉重賣壓打壓指數而重挫，指數早盤最低 43866.72 點，最高 45234.08 點，市場全場成交值估 1.17 兆元。早盤台塑 (1301-TW) 集團股則表現有撑。

台積電昨 (16) 日宣布將 2026 年資本支出大幅上調至 600 億至 640 億美元，較原先規劃的中位數提升 15%。同時表示在亞利桑那州追加 1,000 億美元的投資，可能會再建四個或更多晶圓廠，以滿足主要美國客戶未來多年的強勁需求。至於，面板級先進封裝 CoPoS 的開發進度。目前台積電先進封裝仍以 CoWoS 為主，CoPoS 試驗線正在建置，相關技術及良率預計還需要約一年時間成熟。整體法說正向，但是早盤股價 一度下挫超過 3.5%。

在台股早盤表現上 ，鴻海 (2317-TW) 在平盤之下震盪，聯發科 (2454-TW) 下跌超過 4.5%；而昨日屈居弱勢的 IC 載板類股仍顯弱勢，景碩 (3189-TW)、欣興 (3037-TW) 、臻鼎 - KY(4958-TW) 及南電 (8046-TW) 表現下跌。

7 月 16 日在集中市場 外資賣超 483.35 億元，投信買超 85.49 億元；自營商賣超 33.58 億元，三大法人合計賣超 431.43 億元。而在期貨市場更大動作加碼空單，昨天外資在期貨市場交易口數淨額爲空單 3207 口，未平倉口數淨額爲 84453 口。

資深證券分析師黃漢成指出，目前台股顯現的成交量能萎縮走勢，主要原因在外資對市場看法保守，同時，從電子次族群來看，包括被動元件、IC 載板、功率元件、光通訊的 CPO 等類股輪流下跌，造成上檔套牢者眾，即使股價下跌也乏力承接，台股將呈現高檔箱型整理。

統一證券指出，台積電營收與展望優於預期，僅短期毛利率受新產能持續開出而承壓。同時，市場憂慮長短料問題、CSP 大廠 AI 投資續航力及投資回報率問題，AI 雜音再起。美伊戰爭未解，地緣政治問題干擾油價及美債殖利率，投資市場風險 趨避程度提升。外資期貨空單逾 8.4 萬口，盤面籌碼沉重，

統一證券指出，大盤利空測試進行中，操作建議保持適度資金彈性，避免過度槓桿，選股以流動性佳的中大型 AI 權值股為主。長線追蹤族群：台積電及先進製程 / 封裝、高速傳輸 (含光傳輸)、IC 載板與 PCB、機箱滑軌、功率半導體、特用化學及自行車等族群。

今天早晨美股收盤指數表現上，道瓊工業指數下跌 105.67 點，或 0.20%，報 52,552.97 點。那斯達克指數下跌 387.28 點，或 1.47%，報 25,881.95 點。S&P 500 指數下跌 38.63 點，或 0.51%，報 7,533.77 點。費城半導體指數暴跌 531.39 點，或 4.29%，報 11,867.50 點。

而收盤台股指標的台積電 ADR (TSM-US) 下滑 2.32%，輝達 (NVDA-US) 下跌 2.40%。

news.Title: 韓股今日因「制憲節」休市一天
news.Content:

韓股今日(17日)因「制憲節」休市一天，股市將在下週一(7月20日)重新開盤。

news.Title: Warren Buffett reveals he broke his own investing pattern
news.Content: Legendary investor and now-retired CEO of Berkshire Hathaway ( BRK.A , BRK.B ), Warren Buffett , spent decades teaching investors that knowing what to sidestep is probably as important as finding what to buy. The Oracle of Omaha zeroed in on businesses with predictable demand, durable pricing power, strong cash flow, and advantages built to survive economic cycles. Technology was typically something outside of his comfort zone. The concern was never that the technology lacked value, but that the level of disruption was so extreme that long-term outcomes were tougher to forecast. "If we have a strength, it is in ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Warren Buffett revealed he personally initiated Berkshire Hathaway's significant investment in Alphabet (Google's parent company).
• Berkshire's stake in Alphabet has grown to over $31 billion, including a recent $10 billion private purchase to fund AI development.
• Buffett views Alphabet as a strong business capable of earning high returns on capital, despite its significant AI spending.
• Alphabet's financial profile has shifted due to AI investments, but Buffett believes in its long-term endurance.
• The investment aligns with Buffett's core principles of buying good businesses on sensible terms.
news.Keywords: value investing, strategic investment, artificial intelligence, capital allocation, long-term holding

news.Title: Andy Jassy Says Amazon's Chip Business Already Has $225 Billion in Commitments
news.Content: Amazon (NASDAQ: AMZN) CEO Andy Jassy put a striking number on one of his company's least-discussed businesses this spring. If Amazon's in-house chip operation were a stand-alone company that sold the chips it produces to outside buyers, he said on the company's first-quarter earnings call in April, its annual revenue run rate would be about $50 billion. The business as it actually runs today is no small thing either. Amazon's custom chip unit -- Graviton processors, Trainium artificial intelligence (AI) accelerators, and Nitro networking chips, all deployed inside Amazon Web Services (AWS) -- has an annual ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Amazon CEO Andy Jassy highlighted the company's custom chip business, with an annual revenue run rate above $20 billion.
• The chip unit, including Graviton processors and Trainium AI accelerators, is growing at triple-digit percentage rates.
• Amazon has secured over $225 billion in revenue commitments for its Trainium chips, with major clients like OpenAI and Anthropic.
• The Trainium2 chip offers 30% better price-performance than comparable GPUs and is largely sold out.
• AWS revenue grew 28% in Q1 2026 to $37.6 billion, with operating income up 23% to $14.2 billion, despite significant capital expenditures.
news.Keywords: custom silicon, cloud computing, AI accelerators, revenue commitments, capital expenditures

news.Title: Warren Buffett Just Revealed He -- Not Greg Abel -- Made Berkshire's Big Alphabet Bet. Should You Follow Him In?
news.Content: When Berkshire Hathaway revealed a large Alphabet (NASDAQ: GOOGL)(NASDAQ: GOOG) position last year, the easy assumption was that new CEO Greg Abel was behind it. Warren Buffett , after all, had spent decades steering clear of big technology bets outside of Apple. On Wednesday, Buffett set the record straight. "I initiated it," he told CNBC, adding that not buying the search giant sooner was a mistake. Missed Nvidia in 2009? This Rare Signal Is Flashing Again. ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Warren Buffett confirmed he initiated Berkshire Hathaway's substantial investment in Alphabet, calling it a past mistake not to invest sooner.
• Berkshire's Alphabet stake is now valued at approximately $31 billion, with a recent $10 billion private placement to support AI initiatives.
• Alphabet reported Q1 2026 revenue growth of 22% to $109.9 billion and operating margin expansion to 36.1%.
• Google Cloud revenue surged 63% to $20 billion, with a cloud backlog exceeding $460 billion.
• The company plans significant capital expenditures ($180-$190 billion) for AI development, posing a potential risk if investments do not yield returns.
news.Keywords: investment conviction, artificial intelligence, cloud growth, capital expenditure, valuation

news.Title: 晶片股賣壓延燒，日經指數急挫3.5%，鎧俠暴跌16%，市值較6月高峰腰斬
news.Content:
【財訊快報／陳孟朔】美股晶片與存儲類股隔夜全面重挫，賣壓週五迅速蔓延至日本市場，日經225指數早盤跌幅擴大至3.5%，並較6月高點回落逾一成，正式進入技術性修正區間。投資人持續撤出今年漲幅龐大的AI與半導體概念股，鎧俠、東京威力科創及軟銀集團等權值科技股成為主要提款機。日經225指數早盤一度跌逾3%，其後跌幅擴大至約3.5%；這波回檔承接費城半導體指數週四重挫4.29%、SK海力士ADR暴跌13.69%，以及Sandisk、美光、威騰電子與希捷同步急挫的負面情緒。日本市場對美國晶片股高度敏感，加上日經先前在AI題材帶動下漲幅龐大，短線獲利了結壓力格外集中。

NAND Flash大廠鎧俠(Kioxia Holdings，285A.T)成為跌勢核心，最新報價為52,450日圓，急瀉9,660日圓或15.55%。鎧俠股價6月曾因AI伺服器與存儲需求狂熱升至歷史高位的11.27萬日圓，市值一度突破60兆日圓；隨近期存儲股遭到重新定價，其市值已較6月峰值縮水約一半。市場現階段並非否定NAND與數據中心需求，而是擔憂供應擴張、槓桿資金撤離及估值已提前反映未來數年的樂觀預期。

鎧俠此前一度躍居日本市值最高企業，股價今年曾累計上漲數倍，因此在全球存儲股轉弱時，也承受最劇烈的估值修正。若美國半導體股賣壓未止，日股短線仍可能由晶片設備、存儲與AI相關權值股主導跌勢；市場後續將觀察日經能否守住自6月高點回落10%的修正區，以及鎧俠跌勢是否進一步擴散至更廣泛的日本製造與科技類股。

news.Title: History of Micron: The story behind the computer memory giant
news.Content: J.R. Simplot never dealt with small potatoes. This is a man who dropped out of school in the eighth grade and went on to found the Boise, Idaho-based J.R. Simplot Company, one of the world's largest potato businesses. His wealth soared during World War II when he secured military contracts to supply dehydrated onions and potatoes to the U.S. Armed Forces. After the war, his company pioneered the first commercially viable frozen French fry. Simplot, who liked to say, "when the time is right, you just got to do it," struck a handshake deal with McDonald's ( MCD ) founder ...
news.AI_Summary:
• The article details the history of Micron Technology, a major computer memory manufacturer.
• J.R. Simplot made a significant early investment in Micron, helping it become one of the 'Big Three' memory companies.
• Micron's customer base includes major tech companies like Apple, Nvidia, and Microsoft.
• The company went public in 1984 and has undergone several stock splits.
• Historical context includes trade protection efforts against Japanese competition in the 1980s.
news.Keywords: memory technology, semiconductor industry, historical investment, IPO, market competition

news.Title: JPMorgan Just Reported $21.2 Billion in Q2 Net Income -- Up 41% -- and CEO Jamie Dimon Said the Economy Is "Close to as Good as It Gets."
news.Content:
Some people call big banks the bellwethers of the economy. If that's the case, things may not be all that bad, at least judging by the performance of JPMorgan Chase (NYSE: JPM) in the second quarter.

The nation's largest bank had a record quarter, crushing analysts' estimates. JPMorgan Chase generated a record net income of $21.2 billion, up 41% year over year. Earnings were $7.70 per share, up 47% year over year. On an adjusted basis, the bank earned $16.9 billion, or $6.14 per share. The adjustments were related to special items, which consisted mostly of a one-time $4.6 billion gain from its equity stake in Visa . Analysts had expected earnings of $5.59 per share, so this blew past those estimates.

Missed Nvidia in 2009? This Rare Signal Is Flashing Again. In 2009, a "Double Down" signal flashed for a little-known chipmaker called Nvidia. For the first time in years, that same "Total Conviction" signal is flashing for a company 1/100th the size of Nvidia. Continue »

Revenue also set a record, coming in at $57.3 billion, up 28% year over year and significantly above estimates of $51.1 billion. CEO Jamie Dimon said the firm had record revenue across all lines of business.

"It's getting close to as good as it gets," Dimon said on the earnings call. "We just don't know how long it's going to last."

Improving outlook

It could certainly last a bit longer, as the bank 's credit quality also improved.

Net charge-offs, which are bad loans unlikely to be repaid, fell by $44 billion year over year. In Card Services, the net charge-off rate was down to 3.34% from 3.47% in the first quarter. For the full year, JPMorgan Chase lowered its net charge-off rate in Card Services to 3.2%, down from its previous guidance of 3.4%.

Further, the bank lowered its provision for credit losses, which is money set aside for potential losses. It was down 12% year over year to $2.5 billion.

The bank also raised its net interest income guidance for fiscal 2026 from $103 billion to $105.5 billion.

Investment banking and trading revenue surge

Net interest income rose a robust 10% to $25.6 billion, but the real alpha came from noninterest or fee revenue, which surged 45% to $32.4 billion.

Of JPMʻs three main businesses, Commercial and Investment Banking was the earnings driver. Revenue spiked 27%, and earnings rose 46% in this segment. The biggest boost came from investment banking, which saw revenue spike 45% year over year, and its institutional trading business, where revenue soared 33%. Within the trading business, equity market trading revenue skyrocketed 86% to $6 billion, fueled by a major market rally in April and May.

Story Continues

JPMorgan Chase's Asset and Wealth Management business was also a strong performer, with revenue up 19% and earnings rising 33% year over year.

Its Consumer and Community Banking business lagged, but still had a solid 8% revenue increase with net income ticking up 3%.

JPMorgan Chase stock has climbed about 7% since the earnings were reported on July 14. The stock is now up about 7% per share and is trading at 15 times forward earnings. With its strong outlook and relatively low valuation, JPMorgan Chase stock is a strong buy right now.

news.Title: Michael Burry Says PayPal's $60.50 Buyout Offer Is "Simply Too Low." Here's the Case for a Higher Bid.
news.Content:
PayPal (NASDAQ: PYPL) investors finally got some good news on Wednesday, and it arrived in the form of a takeover offer. Privately held payments company Stripe and private equity firm Advent International have offered $60.50 per share in cash for PayPal, valuing the company at more than $53 billion, CNBC reported.

Shares of the payments specialist soared 17% on the news, closing at $55.52.

Missed Nvidia in 2009? This Rare Signal Is Flashing Again. In 2009, a "Double Down" signal flashed for a little-known chipmaker called Nvidia. For the first time in years, that same "Total Conviction" signal is flashing for a company 1/100th the size of Nvidia. Continue »

Famed investor Michael Burry, best known for his bet against the housing market chronicled in "The Big Short," didn't wait long to weigh in. In a post published Wednesday on his Substack, Cassandra Unchained, Burry, who counts PayPal among his portfolio holdings, wrote that the bid "is at 1.21x IV15 and simply too low."

Is he right? His math, and some of PayPal's own numbers, make a case worth taking seriously.

Burry's math

Burry values companies using intrinsic value, or "IV," estimates. The labels refer to different sets of assumptions, with IV15 representing what a minority investor would pay for shares, by his description.

The problem with the offer, in his view, is that buying a whole company should cost meaningfully more than buying a minority stake.

"A control premium should take any buyout well above IV15, and 21% more is not nearly enough," he wrote.

His estimate of what PayPal is actually worth sits far above the bid.

"True intrinsic value by my methodology is around IV8-IV10," Burry wrote. "IV10 is $75-$80. IV8 is $110-$115. A buyout should be in that range." Adding a control premium to the low end of that range, he figures a winning bid lands at roughly $100 per share.

And he isn't waiting around to find out at current prices.

"$60.50 is just too low. I am not selling, and I believe it is only an opening bid," he wrote.

Burry's valuation figures are one investor's estimates, of course, not appraisals. But you don't need his framework to see why $60.50 could undersell the company.

PayPal's own numbers help the case

The premium is smaller than it looks. The offer came in 28% above Tuesday's closing price of $47.37. But that price followed a brutal stretch for the stock. Shares traded as high as $79.50 within the past year, which means the bid sits about 24% below the stock's own 52-week high.

The offer also looks inexpensive against PayPal's cash generation. The company produced $1.7 billion of adjusted free cash flow in the first quarter alone, up 25% year over year, and it has returned $6 billion to stockholders through share repurchases over the trailing 12 months. At the first quarter's pace of cash generation, a $53 billion price works out to less than eight times a year of adjusted free cash flow. The offer also values PayPal at about 11 times earnings .

Story Continues

To be fair, there's a reason the stock was at $47 in the first place. PayPal's growth has been sluggish. Revenue rose 7% year over year in the first quarter, but transaction margin dollars, a key profitability measure for the company, rose just 3%.

Active accounts came in at 439 million, up only 1% from a year earlier and down slightly from the prior quarter -- user growth has stalled. And management's full-year guidance calls for adjusted earnings per share ranging from a low-single-digit decline to slightly positive.

This is not a business commanding a growth premium. But it doesn't need to be for the bid to look light.

The negotiation itself could push the price higher, too. Stripe was reportedly interested in PayPal as early as February, and the current offer includes roughly $50 billion in committed bank financing. PayPal hasn't responded yet -- CNBC reported that the company's board will meet as soon as Monday, July 20, to discuss the offer. If the board rejects $60.50 as inadequate, the bidders can walk away or raise.

Notably, the market isn't fully convinced. At $55.52, shares trade about 8% below the offer price, suggesting investors see some chance the deal stalls or falls apart. If talks collapse, the stock could give back much of Wednesday's gain.

Ultimately, I think Burry's core point holds up. The offer prices PayPal's free cash flow cheaply, and it sits well below where the stock traded a year ago. Whether that produces a higher bid is out of shareholders' hands. But with the board's response possibly just days away, current shareholders arguably have little reason to rush to the exits at a price below the offer itself.

news.Title: Should You Buy Tesla Stock Before July 22?
news.Content:
Tesla (NASDAQ: TSLA) is set to report financials for the second quarter of 2026 on Wednesday, July 22. This is an important time for investors, as the business will provide them with performance updates that can inform portfolio moves.

This "Magnificent Seven" stock has meaningfully underperformed the market in 2026 (down 12.4% compared to the S&P 500 's 10.6% gain). But is Tesla a buy before its upcoming financial release?

Missed Nvidia in 2009? This Rare Signal Is Flashing Again. In 2009, a "Double Down" signal flashed for a little-known chipmaker called Nvidia. For the first time in years, that same "Total Conviction" signal is flashing for a company 1/100th the size of Nvidia. Continue »

To be clear, investors shouldn't make investment decisions solely on the basis of front-running a company's earnings report. This is a short-sighted mentality. It's incredibly rare that any information a business reveals related to a single quarter has a material impact on its long-term investment thesis.

That said, there is still valuable data available to investors to assess whether a company is performing well. In Tesla's case, automotive revenue growth and gross margin, the outlook for capital expenditures, and CEO Elon Musk's commentary on Robotaxi and Optimus developments are incredibly important.

The question, though, isn't if investors should buy Tesla stock before July 22. The question is whether this stock is worth buying and holding for the next five years.

With this framework in mind, I believe investors are better off avoiding Tesla. The stock's extreme price-to-earnings ratio of 358 underscores how astronomical the market's expectations are, creating an asymmetric opportunity skewed to the downside.

The company deserves credit for tackling ambitious projects that can have a global impact. However, the current setup is not compelling.

Missed Nvidia in 2009? This Rare Signal Is Flashing Again

In 2009, a "Double Down" signal flashed for a little-known chipmaker called Nvidia. If you'd invested $5,000 then, you'd be sitting on $2,759,194 today.*

Now, for the first time in years, that same "Total Conviction" signal is flashing for a company 1/100th the size of Nvidia. It's a key player in the $1.8 trillion space race, and with the stock recently sitting 20% off its highs, the window to get in early is closing fast.

Continue »

*Stock Advisor returns as of July 13, 2026

Neil Patel has no position in any of the stocks mentioned. The Motley Fool has positions in and recommends Tesla. The Motley Fool has a disclosure policy .

news.Title: Chipmakers put pressure on equity indexes globally, oil dips
news.Content:
By Sinéad Carew and Marc Jones

NEW YORK/LONDON, July 16 (Reuters) - Equity indexes around the world fell on Thursday as investors offloaded heavy-weight chip stocks while the U.S. dollar and Treasury yields rose after the latest economic releases and as the Middle East war intensified.

Chip stocks ‌fell from Asia to the U.S., as higher-than-expected 77% earnings growth from Taiwanese chip manufacturing giant TSMC was not enough to impress investors who ‌have heavily leaned into technology stocks related to artificial intelligence.

More from Yahoo Scout

Why are AI-related technology stocks facing increased selling pressure?

How are Middle East tensions affecting oil and commodity markets?

Why did global chip stocks fall despite TSMC's strong earnings?

What drove the US dollar and Treasury yield movements?

The U.S. second-quarter reporting season started off well, with analyst expectations for quarterly earnings growth increasing to 24.8% on Wednesday from 23.7% last week, ​according to LSEG. But high expectations could result in short-term weakness, said Tony Welch, chief investment officer at SignatureFD.

"When you've a lot of optimism in the market you need everything to go right. Any piece of negative news can throw the market off," he said. "There's a lot of confidence built in right now. It's not a bad thing in itself but it does create a high hurdle for market prices to keep going higher."

On Wall Street, the Dow Jones Industrial Average fell 105.67 points, or 0.2%, to 52,552.97, the S&P ‌500 fell 38.63 points, or 0.5%, to 7,533.77 and the ⁠Nasdaq Composite fell 387.28 points, or 1.5%, to 25,881.95.

MSCI's gauge of stocks across the globe fell 6.49 points, or 0.6%, to 1,121.65. The pan-European STOXX 600 index closed up 0.16%.

South Korea's technology-heavy KOSPI index fell more than 6%, while Japan's Nikkei closed nearly 3% ⁠lower. The Philadelphia semiconductor index tumbled 4.3%, its second straight daily loss.

"That tells you the AI trade isn't being priced on growth anymore. It's being priced on perfection. Any earnings report that's merely great, instead of flawless, gets sold," said Gene Goldman, chief investment officer at Cetera in El Segundo, California.

OIL EASES EVEN AS WAR ESCALATES

Iran and the United States ​exchanged ​fire on Thursday, intensifying attacks that have persisted since the weekend, largely unraveling the truce that ​paused fighting last month. While the two countries wrestle for ‌control of the Strait of Hormuz, Iran signalled that it could prod Houthi allies in Yemen to close the Bab al-Mandeb Strait at the mouth of the Red Sea, another key oil route.

Still, oil futures edged lower, with U.S. crude settling down 0.8%, or 65 cents, to $78.95 a barrel while Brent settled at $84.23 per barrel, down 0.85%, or 72 cents.

Story Continues

U.S. retail sales increased 0.2% in June, in line with expectations, as lower gasoline prices weighed on receipts at service stations, though consumers continued to support underlying spending. Weekly initial jobless claims dropped to a seasonally adjusted 208,000, below economists' 217,000 estimate.

U.S. Treasury yields were modestly higher after those figures did ‌little to alter investors' expectations for the path of interest rates from the Federal Reserve.

The ​yield on benchmark U.S. 10-year notes rose 1.44 basis points to 4.559%, from 4.545% late on Wednesday ​while the 30-year bond yield rose 0.25 basis points to 5.0855%.

The 2-year ​note yield, which typically moves in step with interest rate expectations for the Federal Reserve, rose 2.55 basis points to 4.154%, from ‌4.128%.

The dollar edged higher against major peers, though was still ​near a one-month low, reflecting expectations that the ​U.S. economy will remain resilient and that the Fed will hold rates steady this month.

The dollar index, which measures the greenback against a basket of currencies including the yen and the euro, rose 0.3% to 100.74, while the euro fell 0.2% at $1.1441.

Against the Japanese yen, the dollar strengthened 0.1% ​to 162.37. Sterling weakened 0.5% to $1.3475, slipping from the two-month high ‌it reached on Wednesday.

In precious metals, gold fell to a two-week low after rising the prior two sessions. Spot gold fell 2.1% to $3,976.24 ​an ounce while spot silver fell 3.8% to $55.56 an ounce.

(Reporting by Sinéad Carew, Caroline Valetkevitch, Chuck Mikolajczak in New York, Marc Jones in ​London, Stella Qiu in Sydney; Editing by Thomas Derpinghaus, Joe Bavier and David Gaffen)

news.Ticker: META, GOOGL, AAPL, TSLA, NVDA, MSFT, AMZN
news.Title: The "Magnificent Seven" Are at Their Lowest Relative Valuations in a Decade. My 3 Favorite Mag 7 Stocks to Buy.
news.Content:
The so-called "Magnificent Seven" group of stocks -- Apple , Alphabet (NASDAQ: GOOGL) (NASDAQ: GOOG), Amazon (NASDAQ: AMZN), Meta Platforms (NASDAQ: META), Microsoft , Nvidia , and Tesla -- has long traded at a premium to the S&P 500 index. For the past decade, the group has generally traded at a P/E about 30% above the benchmark index, but that premium has recently fallen to its lowest level ever, closer to just 10% above the benchmark. Much of that premium can be attributed to Tesla, which trades at a trailing P/E of over 350.

With the Magnificent Seven trading at its lowest-ever relative valuation, let's look at my three favorite stocks in the group to buy right now.

Missed Nvidia in 2009? This Rare Signal Is Flashing Again. In 2009, a "Double Down" signal flashed for a little-known chipmaker called Nvidia. For the first time in years, that same "Total Conviction" signal is flashing for a company 1/100th the size of Nvidia. Continue »

Amazon

The market share leader in both e-commerce and cloud computing , Amazon is one of the most underappreciated stocks in the market today. The stock has been a laggard over the last five years, up only around 35%. However, the company itself has been making big strides during this time.

While it has gotten little credit for it, Amazon has become the world's largest manufacturer and operator of robots, all of which run on its DeepFleet AI model. It's also adopted AI to help optimize things like delivery routes and inventory management. This has all made the company much more efficient and helped drive strong operating leverage in its e-commerce business.

Meanwhile, the company is seeing accelerating revenue growth in its cloud computing business, and partnerships with Anthropic and OpenAI should help this continue. Amazon also has a strong chip business, having developed its own AI accelerators and central processing units (CPUs), which help give it a cost advantage.

Trading at a forward P/E of 25.5 times 2027 analyst estimates, the stock is attractively valued and is a solid long-term buy.

Alphabet

Alphabet is not just a search giant; it's a strong collection of leading and emerging businesses. It's also become the most complete AI player, having developed its own world-class chips with its Tensor Processing Units (TPUs) and a frontier AI model in Gemini.

TPUs are Alphabet's secret sauce, giving it a big cost advantage over competitors that rely largely on Nvidia's expensive graphics processing units (GPUs). It uses its chips to train its AI model at a much lower cost than rivals, while it also lets it run inference much more cheaply. Its TPUs are so well regarded that Anthropic has placed huge orders for them.

Story Continues

The company's cloud unit is seeing rapid growth, with revenue surging 63% in Q1, while it has embedded Gemini within Google search, helping drive query and revenue growth. Alphabet also owns YouTube and has a potential future growth driver with its Waymo robotaxi business, which is aggressively expanding to new cities across the U.S.

Trading at a forward P/E of 25 times 2027 estimates, Alphabet is one of my favorite stocks to own for the long term given its built-in advantages.

Meta Platforms

A social media giant, Meta has been one of the best companies at using AI to help drive growth in its core business. It's developed its own models to help improve its recommendation algorithm, which is feeding users more of the content they are interested in and keeping them on its apps longer. At the same time, it's using AI to help advertisers better connect with and convert customers, which is driving up ad demand and prices.

Meta grew its revenue at a brisk 33% pace last quarter, yet the stock only trades at a forward P/E of 18 times 2027 estimates. The company is also just starting to serve ads on its popular messaging platform, WhatsApp, and its new social media site, Threads, which should add another growth driver.

The biggest knock on the stock has been its aggressive AI infrastructure spending, but Meta looking to start a cloud computing service, given the high demand for compute power, helps allay those fears. Meanwhile, its latest Muse Spark 1.1 model has drawn strong praise.

The stock looks way undervalued given its growth and prospects.

news.Title: This GLP-1 Stock Could Double by 2030. Here's Why
news.Content:
Over the past five years, Viking Therapeutics ' (NASDAQ: VKTX) shares have climbed more than 500% as the company has made significant progress with its leading pipeline candidates. The clinical-stage biotech is looking to establish itself in the fast-growing GLP-1 drug market. If it can make headway toward that goal through the end of the decade, Viking Therapeutics might once again deliver market-beating returns. Here is why the stock could double by 2030.

Missed Nvidia in 2009? This Rare Signal Is Flashing Again. In 2009, a "Double Down" signal flashed for a little-known chipmaker called Nvidia. For the first time in years, that same "Total Conviction" signal is flashing for a company 1/100th the size of Nvidia. Continue »

A large and growing opportunity

Demand for GLP-1 products is soaring. Thanks to breakthroughs in the field and a large addressable opportunity in niches such as diabetes and weight management, the market for GLP-1 drugs could reach $190 billion by 2035, according to some estimates. For context, it was worth about $79 billion last year. While Eli Lilly (NYSE: LLY) and Novo Nordisk (NYSE: NVO) currently dominate this area, a first-mover advantage isn't everything. There is room for more differentiated GLP-1 medicines.

That's what Viking Therapeutics is working on. The company's pipeline includes subcutaneous VK2735, an investigational medicine currently in phase 3 clinical trials. The biotech company is also working on an oral version of VK2735 that could start late-stage clinical trials by the end of the year. Further, Viking Therapeutics said it would start phase 1 studies for another product, VK3019, in the second quarter.

While VK2735 mimics the actions of the GLP-1 and GIP hormones, VK3019 is a dual amylin and calcitonin agonist. This differentiated product could help Viking Therapeutics maintain a strong position in the weight-loss market as more GLP-1 medicines gain approval. The biotech could also seek to combine VK2735 and VK3019, thereby attacking obesity from multiple pathways, potentially yielding efficacy far stronger than current options.

The bull case for Viking Therapeutics

What would it take for Viking Therapeutics to double by 2030? The biggest catalyst will be progress with its leading candidate, subcutaneous VK2735. The medicine's ongoing phase 3 studies should be completed within the next 12 to 18 months -- and, if the results from these trials are strong, the therapy could earn approval by mid to late 2028 or so. However, clinical successes don't necessarily translate to the kind of commercial success investors want. The best-case scenario for Viking Therapeutics is for VK2735 to post phase 3 results that at least rival those of the current leader in the obesity drug market, Zepbound.

Story Continues

That would likely send the stock price soaring, as it would set Viking Therapeutics on a trajectory to generate billions in annual sales from its leading product. Progress with oral VK2735 through the next few years will also be important. Oral weight-loss medicines are expanding the market by attracting mostly patients who had never taken GLP-1s before. But their efficacy pales in comparison with that of subcutaneous medicines. Oral VK2735 posted highly impressive weight-loss numbers in a phase 2 study, but the study had high discontinuation rates due to the medicine's side effects.

If Viking Therapeutics can find a way to mitigate oral VK2735's side effects -- perhaps with a gentler titration schedule -- while maintaining its strong efficacy, that could be a game changer for the company. Lastly, encouraging early stage results for VK3019 may also slightly move the needle for Viking Therapeutics.

Hedge your bets

Could Viking Therapeutics really double by 2030? Yes, provided everything goes right. But how likely is that? In my view, there is a good chance subcutaneous VK2735 posts strong phase 3 results, but if it fails to do so, the stock will fall off a cliff. It will likely struggle to recover, especially since the company is currently valued at $4.4 billion, which isn't at all insignificant for a clinical-stage biotech. There is also the possibility that the GLP-1 market will not grow as quickly as some analysts currently project, or that there will be far more competition from pharmaceutical giants than expected.

Any of these outcomes could lead to much higher expectations for Viking Therapeutics' upcoming data readouts and make it harder for the company to impress the market. The bottom line is that Viking Therapeutics is on the risky side. Investors comfortable with volatility may add a small position in the stock to a well-diversified portfolio, but risk-averse investors will want to look elsewhere.

news.Title: SK Hynix Makes Up Over a Quarter of South Korea's Stock Market. If You'd Invested $5,000 in the Memory Giant 5 Years Ago, Here's How Much It Would Be Worth Today.
news.Content:
Investors should always keep an open mind when looking for new investment ideas, whether that means exploring uncommon sectors or even countries they don't live in.

SK Hynix (KOSE: A000660) (NASDAQ: SKHY) is proof of that. The South Korean memory chip company has proven to be a key player in the artificial intelligence (AI) supply chain and has generated phenomenal gains for South Korean shareholders.

Missed Nvidia in 2009? This Rare Signal Is Flashing Again. In 2009, a "Double Down" signal flashed for a little-known chipmaker called Nvidia. For the first time in years, that same "Total Conviction" signal is flashing for a company 1/100th the size of Nvidia. Continue »

In fact, SK Hynix now accounts for over 25% of the Korea Composite Stock Price Index , South Korea's benchmark. If you'd invested $5,000 in SK Hynix five years ago (something U.S. investors would have had great difficulty doing until recently), here's how much you'd have today.

Why SK Hynix stock has done so well

SK Hynix is not a new company, having been founded in 1983 as Hyundai Electronics. The company is one of the largest manufacturers of dynamic random-access memory (DRAM) and NAND flash memory, which have long powered consumer electronics.

This technology has become a key part of AI because DRAM and NAND chips play critical roles in feeding data to the general-purpose graphics processing units (GPUs) made by companies like Nvidia to train large language models (LLMs). As GPU clusters and data centers have scaled, so too has the need for DRAM and NAND.

NAND serves as permanent storage for data that GPUs can access at any time, such as operating systems and data sets. It's not as fast as DRAM, but it provides cheaper access to storage. DRAM is temporary storage, meaning the data is lost when power is off, but it's much faster and key to enabling GPUs to pull in as much data per second as they do.

Memory companies have historically been viewed as cyclical because whenever there is a surge in demand for DRAM and NAND, it takes time for memory companies to catch up, making it difficult to predict when the supply-and-demand balance will even out. Oftentimes, companies will ramp up supply too much, leading prices to eventually fall. However, Wall Street analysts believe demand for DRAM and NAND will be constrained for the next few years, leading memory prices to climb through 2027 and even potentially into 2028.

In the first quarter of 2026, SK Hynix held the second-largest market share in the global NAND and DRAM markets, with 18% and 29%, respectively. The company also reported record first-quarter earnings results. Revenue tripled year over year, while operating profit rose more than 400%.

Story Continues

Earlier this month, SK Hynix raised $26.5 billion in funds by listing 178 million American depositary receipts (ADRs) on the Nasdaq exchange under the ticker SKHY. Ten ADRs are equal to one share of the South Korean stock.

If you'd invested $5,000 five years ago

All the big memory companies have done incredibly well, and at one point, SK Hynix topped $1 trillion in market cap. According to Alphabet 's Google, SK Hynix stock is up over 1,418% in the last five years. That means a $5,000 investment five years ago would be worth $75,900 today, crushing the returns achieved by investing in any major stock index.

Now, as long as memory remains in a supply crunch and companies like SK Hynix can maintain or even raise memory prices, the stock will do well going forward in both South Korea and in the U.S. There's also an argument that the AI supercycle could create a new environment for memory companies, leading to longer contracts and less cyclicality in these stocks.

I'd caution investors from getting too bullish and thinking that this cycle will be different. It could be, yes, but there's also a very real possibility that, while this cycle looks different, it ends the same way as in the past.

Betting too much on memory companies also makes investors beholden to the AI trade. Should the AI trade suffer a big setback or even roadblocks, stocks like SK Hynix could take a big hit.

That's why I wouldn't make SK Hynix too large a position in your portfolio right now. However, holding some exposure is certainly OK, as there could still be room to run for these stocks now and over the longer term.

news.Title: 3 AI Stocks Poised to Rebound After Slow Starts to the Year
news.Content:
Artificial intelligence has been the defining trade of the 2020s, and many of the top performers are up so far this year. However, there are a few laggards that investors have missed. These companies have continued to strengthen their fundamentals even as investors look the other way.

Stocks tend to reward people in the long run if they continue to gain market share. This general rule makes these three AI stocks look destined for rebounds.

Missed Nvidia in 2009? This Rare Signal Is Flashing Again. In 2009, a "Double Down" signal flashed for a little-known chipmaker called Nvidia. For the first time in years, that same "Total Conviction" signal is flashing for a company 1/100th the size of Nvidia. Continue »

1. Synopsys

Synopsys (NASDAQ: SNPS) designs AI chips. Its Electronic Design Automation enables it to create chips at scale, and its intellectual property keeps most competitors out of its industry. It also ensures customers are deeply involved across Synopsys' product portfolio, which enables chip creation.

The leading chipmakers use Synopsys, and the company recently secured an expanded partnership and $2 billion investment from Nvidia .

This strategic position makes Synopsys' 12% year-to-date decline all the more jarring, especially as the company continues to gain market share. It reported 42% year-over-year revenue growth in its fiscal 2026 second quarter and raised its full-year revenue guidance.

The company's landmark $35 billion Ansys acquisition helped it gain more market share, but it's why margins have been tight in the short run. Amortization costs have been expensive, but once those expenses start to wind down, Synopsys is well-positioned to deliver higher margins for investors. It's an attractive long-term buying opportunity, but it's mired in a short-term correction.

2. Constellation Energy

Constellation Energy (NASDAQ: CEG) is the leading producer of clean, reliable energy. It has 55 gigawatts of capacity from nuclear, natural gas, and other energy sources, providing around-the-clock baseload generation for AI data centers. It gets to sign long-term deals with tech companies without pouring substantial capital into building data centers.

The company explained why revenue surged 61% year over year in the first quarter and told investors to expect "strong, visible cash flow" throughout the year. A 22.3 P/E ratio ought to be the icing on the cake, but shares are currently down by roughly 30% year to date.

Some investors didn't like it when the company acquired U.S. power producer Calpine for $16.4 billion. The stock is still down from when Constellation Energy announced the acquisition in January 2025. Investors saw it as a move that increased debt and dilution while shifting resources toward cyclical gas rather than nuclear energy.

Story Continues

Constellation Energy is still trending in the right direction and is expected to guide for 20% EPS growth from 2026 to 2029. Multiyear tailwinds should boost the value of Constellation Energy's energy portfolio and can help the stock navigate past this current correction.

3. Microsoft

Not every hyperscaler has outperformed the S&P 500 (SNPINDEX: ^GSPC) this year, and Microsoft (NASDAQ: MSFT) is one of them. It's down by more than 15% year to date and has received some price target cuts from Wall Street analysts, but the doom and gloom is a bit overdone at this point.

Microsoft is a leader in cloud computing, which positions it well for the AI boom. The tech giant's AI business more than doubled its annual revenue run rate year over year in its fiscal 2026 third quarter. The overall numbers were solid. Revenue increased by 18% year over year, and net income jumped by 20% year over year.

Those growth rates go along with a 23 P/E ratio. That valuation, combined with Microsoft's long-term opportunities, suggests that the stock is undervalued at current levels.

Microsoft executive vice president Amy Hood cited "growing demand for Microsoft Cloud" in the most recent quarter. Cloud revenue grew faster than the overall business, accounting for more than half of Microsoft's Q3 FY26 revenue. This trend can accelerate overall revenue across Microsoft's business.

The specialty chip foundry plans to invest $3 billion to expand its manufacturing footprint in Japan, supported by $1 billion in grants from the Japanese government. The dual-track expansion will boost production of silicon photonics, silicon-germanium, and advanced packaging technologies that are increasingly critical for artificial intelligence (AI), ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Tower Semiconductor (TSEM) is investing $3 billion to expand its manufacturing footprint in Japan, supported by $1 billion in government grants, to boost production of critical AI technologies.
• The expansion will increase capacity for silicon photonics, silicon-germanium, and advanced packaging, essential for AI, data centers, and communications.
• The first phase targets a 300mm silicon photonics plant by Q4 2027, with a second phase for a new 300mm fab.

Despite their different business models, both companies are benefiting from the same wave of AI-driven capital spending. The AI infrastructure investment cycle is gaining momentum as hyperscalers, cloud providers and enterprises continue expanding their AI capabilities. According to Mordor Intelligence, the global AI infrastructure market is projected to reach $202.5 billion by ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Lumentum (LITE) is benefiting from the AI infrastructure buildout by supplying advanced optical networking components for high-speed data transmission.
• In Q3 fiscal 2026, Lumentum's Components revenues grew 77.3% and Systems revenues jumped 121.1% due to strong AI demand.

• Lumentum's optical portfolio, including EMLs and cloud transceivers, is essential for AI networking and data center interconnects.

news.Title: 大摩：博通將長期壟斷Google TPU約80%的市場
news.Content:
摩根士丹利 (大摩) 分析師 Joseph Moore 在最新的研究報告中，強力為晶片大廠博通 (AVGO-US) 辯護，重申其「增持」(Overweight) 評級及 502 美元的目標價。

報告強調，博通是人工智慧 (AI) 浪潮中僅次於輝達 (NVDA-US) 的核心贏家，並預測其將長期壟斷 Google(GOOGL-US)TPU 約 80% 的市場份額。

針對近期市場擔心聯發科 (2454-TW) 可能侵蝕博通在 Google 客製化晶片業務份額的疑慮，摩根士丹利認為這些擔憂「言之過早」且「過度反應」。雖然聯發科確實參與了 Google 3 奈米 TPU 的設計項目，且 Google 確實有分散供應商以降低成本的動機，但摩根士丹利指出，聯發科的介入雖然「真實」但「不具顛覆性」。

報告分析，聯發科在先進封裝 (如 CoWoS) 和產能規模上仍面臨挑戰，而博通則憑藉著已鎖定的高頻寬記憶體 (HBM) 供應合約、卓越的封裝能力以及龐大的生產規模，穩固了其競爭優勢。聯發科自身的長期目標也僅設定在 15% 至 20% 的市占率，與摩根士丹利預期博通保留 80% 份額的觀點吻合。

在財務預測方面，摩根士丹利展現了極大的信心，預計博通 2027 財年的 AI 相關營收將達到約 1,200 億美元。其中，TPU 相關業務貢獻預計占 800 億美元。

隨著多個新客製化 ASIC 客戶預計在 2027 年下半年開始放量，雖然 TPU 占總 AI 營收的比重可能降至 60%，但這反映了博通在客群多樣化上的成功，包括與 Meta、Apple 及 Anthropic 的深度合作。

儘管博通 2026 年以來的股價表現落後於半導體類股指數 (SOXX-US)，且較 6 月的歷史高點回撤約 19% 至 22%，但摩根士丹利認為這種表現與其強勁的 AI 成長軌跡並不匹配。

摩根士丹利總結認為，博通擁有領先的 ASIC 技術、強大的網路專長及值得信賴的管理團隊，是目前半導體產業中最具吸引力的成長故事之一。

news.Title: 三大法人賣超431億元 齊買力積電近6萬張 外資大動作加碼台指期空單
news.Content:
台北股市今 (16) 日盤中在觀望台積電 (2330-TW) 下午法說會的訊息同時，大盤成交量能也呈現明顯萎縮，終場集中市場大盤下跌 6.61 點，以 45624.98 點作收，成交值明顯萎縮爲 9008.39 億元，三大法人賣超 431.43 億元。外資主要賣超南亞 (1303-TW) 5.26 萬張，同時減碼記憶體股旺宏 (2337-TW)、南亞科(2408-TW) 、華邦電(2344-TW) 各 3.4 萬、2.19 萬及 7487 張。

7 月 16 日在集中市場 外資賣超 483.35 億元，投信買超 85.49 億元；自營商賣超 33.58 億元，三大法人合計賣超 431.43 億元。但三大法人同步加碼力積電 (6770-TW) 合計 59355 張，而在期貨市場更大動作加碼空單，今天外資在期貨市場交易口數淨額爲空單 4896 口，未平倉口數淨額爲 84453 口。

集中市場今天外資買超較多的個股爲，力積電、臺企銀、南茂、中信金、寶成、中鋼、台泥、凱基金、中華電、遠東新；賣超較多的個股爲，南亞、旺宏、南亞科、聯電、康舒、友達、臻鼎 - KY、華邦電、中工、欣興。

集中市場今天投信買超較多的個股爲，南亞、力積電、台新新光金、台塑化、欣興、日月光投控、統一、上海商銀、遠東新、華通。賣超較多的個股爲，華航、聯電、中信金、華邦電、永豐金、緯創、長榮航、華南金、兆豐金、英業達。

集中市場今天自營商買超較多的個股爲，聯電、力積電、永豐金、台塑化、兆豐金、華邦電、群創、台塑、鴻海、億光；賣超較多的個股爲，亞泥、廣達、英業達、台橡、中工、漢翔。

資深證券分析師黃漢成指出，目前台股顯現的成交量能萎縮走勢，主要原因在外資對市場看法保守，同時，從電子次族群來看，包括被動元件、IC 載板、功率元件、光通訊的 CPO 等類股輪流下跌，造成上檔套牢者眾，即使股價下跌也乏力承接，兩大因素造成台股的量縮下跌格局，目前看集中市場指數支撐在 43500-43700 點。但上檔 10 日線及月線的龐大壓力，也讓盤勢形成高檔震盪格局。

news.Title: 《陸股》滬指失守3900點、跌1.85%；科創50跌4.02%
news.Content:
MoneyDJ新聞 2026-07-16 17:15:26 數位內容中心 發佈

中國A股今(16)日走弱，上證指數失守3900點、收於3882.41點，下跌1.85%，日K連二黑；深證成指收於14488.65點，下跌1.97%；創業板指收於3692.46點，下跌2.95%；科創50指數收於1846.88點，大跌4.02%。盤勢呈現低開回升後再度走低，科技成長類股回檔，對大盤形成明顯拖累。

成交量方面，滬市今成交1兆1,242億元(人民幣,下同)，深市成交1兆2,794億元，兩市成交額2兆4,036億元，較前一交易日量縮約6%。

從盤面上來看，半導體、存儲晶片與先進封裝族群回調明顯，成為壓抑指數的主因，算力晶片、半導體設備與電子化學品方向也同步走弱，而油氣與煤炭族群亦偏弱。相較之下，醫藥族群逆勢活躍，影視院線族群表現突出，邊緣AI概念盤中走揚，高壓氧艙概念也位居漲幅前列。

《陸股》滬指漲1.36%、日K翻紅；創業板指漲逾3.4%

《陸股》滬指漲1.65%重返4000點；科創50大漲8.41%

news.Title: 【公告】瑞昱115年第2季線上法人說明會
news.Content:
日 期：2026年07月16日

公司名稱：瑞昱 (2379)

主 旨：115年第2季線上法人說明會

發言人：黃依瑋

說 明：

符合條款第四條第XX款：12

news.Title: 〈陸股盤後〉午後跌幅擴大 三大指數一度跌超過2%
news.Content:
中國 A 股三大指數 16 日（周四）集體開低，早盤回暖後，跌幅明顯收窄，午後兩市重返跌勢，跌幅明顯擴大，三大指數一度跌超過 2%。

上證綜指 16 日收盤跌 1.85%，報 3,882.41 點；深證成指跌 1.97%，報 14,488.65 點；創業板指跌 2.95%，報 3,692.46 點。

滬深兩市成交總額為人民幣 2.4036 兆元，較前一交易日減少 1,676 億元。

據《澎湃新聞》報導，財信證券認為，總體來看，大盤整體表現還是低於預期。一方面是體現在海外科技資產反彈的背景下，A 股科創方向反而出現較大負反饋，硬科技線短期波動加劇態勢未改善，這可能會拖累市場情緒，導致後續大盤反彈動力以及其他板塊持續性受限。

另一方面則體現在全市場成交額連續縮量，反映出資金信心相對不足。

因此，短期內市場可能仍以震盪築底為主，需出現更加積極信號，如大盤放量長陽、三大指數重回 5 日和 10 日均線上、熱點板塊行情持續性改善等其中之一，才能基本確認大盤具備上攻動能。

在此之前，投資者還是以適當控制倉位為主，以震盪行情觀察為主的思路參與市場輪動，待市場出現積極信號，再加大參與力道。

財信證券說，中期來看，7 月份處於市場過渡階段，預計市場將處於較大波動狀態，建議投資人以均衡配置為主。

news.Title: 《日股》AI/半導體權值股領跌 日經挫2.79%
news.Content:
MoneyDJ新聞 2026-07-16 16:07:02 數位內容中心 發佈

日股今(16)日走跌。日經225指數收於66,835.54點，下跌1,915.97點或2.79%，盤中最低觸及66,499.49點；東證股價指數(TOPIX)收於4,028.79點，下跌59.33點或1.45%。盤面主軸明確，AI與半導體相關權值股遭到集中調節，使指數快速回檔，市場情緒也因避險升溫而轉趨保守。

外部環境方面，日股開盤後即承接前一交易日美國半導體股走弱的壓力，加上韓國KOSPI指數盤中重跌，進一步加劇亞洲半導體鏈的連鎖賣壓，日股因此難以擺脫同步走弱的格局。

權值股方面，日經225指數跌勢主要由半導體與AI權值股主導，愛德萬測試(6857.JP)、東京威力科創(8035.JP)、軟銀集團(9984.JP)、Kioxia Holdings(285A.JP)與IBIDEN(4062.JP)成為主要拖累，Lasertec(6920.JP)、Disco(6146.JP)等相關個股也承受明顯賣壓。

相較之下，醫藥、運輸設備、紙漿與紙、零售等類股表現相對抗跌，帶動中外製藥(4519.JP)、第一三共(4568.JP)、Toyota Motor(7203.JP)、Honda Motor(7267.JP)、良品計劃(7453.JP)、Terumo(4543.JP)、Recruit Holdings(6098.JP)等個股逆勢走揚。資金明顯轉向內需與防禦型標的，成為今日少數支撐盤面的力量。

《日股》半導體領軍，日經漲逾千點/收漲1.49%

《日股》日經震盪收低、TOPIX創高

news.Title: 外資加碼日股；日人大買海外中長債、今年第3高
news.Content:
MoneyDJ新聞 2026-07-16 13:35:29 蔡承啟 發佈

外資3週來首度加碼日股，而日本投資人(日人)連4週加碼海外股票，且大買海外中長債、週買超額創今年來第3高水準。

日本財務省16日公佈統計數據指出，上週(7月5-11日)外資在日本股市買超7,456億日圓，3週來首度加碼日股；2026年迄今(截至7月11日為止)外資買超日股的累計金額擴大至9兆783億日圓。

上週外資買超日本中長期債券金額為4,998億日圓，6週來首度站在買方；賣超日本短期債券金額為5,897億日圓，3週來第2度站在賣方。

總計外資上週流入日本金融市場的資金總額為6,557億日圓，6週來首度呈現淨流入。

上週日本投資人(日人)在海外股市買超1,962億日圓，連續第4週加碼海外股票；2026年迄今(截至7月11日為止)日人買超海外股票的累計金額擴大至3,177億日圓。

日人上週買超海外中長期債券金額達1兆901億日圓，3週來首度站在買方，週買超額為9週來(5月3-9日當週以來、買超1兆6,443億日圓)首度突破1兆日圓、創今年來第3高水準；買超海外短期債券金額為7,457億日圓，4週來第3度站在買方。

總計日人上週流入海外金融市場(=流出日本金融市場)的資金總額為2兆320億日圓，連續第4週呈現淨流入。

(圖片來源：Shutterstock)

news.Title: 聯電ADR15日上漲1.08美元漲幅4.53%折台股160.43元
Cisco Took 25 Years to Make Investors Whole Again After Its Dot-Com Crash. Here's What That Means for Traders Buying NVIDIA Now.
news.Content: There's always a strange nervousness that comes with trying to price a company that no one doubts anymore. Unlike those messy, "misunderstood" names that are easy to dig into, a company everyone already likes gives you almost nothing to push back against… except for maybe some far-fetched speculation. And that's exactly why it's the process of digging into those messy names where investors usually find their edge. I'm not talking about those speculative artificial intelligence (AI) infrastructure bets that are clearly overvalued, but those well-established names with the deepest pockets that even the most conservative investors pour money into. More ...
news.AI_Summary:
• The article discusses the difficulty in valuing companies that are already widely admired, using Cisco (CSCO) as an example of a stock that took 25 years to recover from the dot-com crash.
• It warns against following the herd into 'Wall Street darlings' where future growth may already be priced in.
• The concept of 'any price' being worth it for high-quality companies is contrasted with the reality of potentially average returns.
• Historical examples like the Nifty Fifty stocks in the 1970s are used to illustrate how high valuations can lead to disappointment even for strong businesses.
news.Keywords: valuation, market sentiment, historical analysis, investment trap, stock price
